description:
Through field and forest is the Schnitzel Hunt.  Up to 10 hunters pursue the goal.  This isn't so simple as there are many false trails, detours, and adventures cause many delays.  The game takes luck and skill to win.

---

Kreuz und quer durch Feld und Wald führt eine Schnizeljagd.  Bis zur 10 jäger versuchen, rasch zum Ziel zu kommen. Dies ist gar nicht so einfach, den viele Fehlspuren, Umwegen, und allerei Abenteuer verursachen immer wieder Auftenhalte.  Es gehört neben dem notwendigen Glück auch eine Geschichtlichskeit dazu, als Sieger ans Ziel zu kommen.

A ladybug flies through the flowering seasons, from winter flurries to the end of Fall.  Through his travels he (and all of the players) learn about many flowers and about how a wonderfully pretty a planned garden can be.

---

Ein Marienkäfer fliegt durch das Blumenjahr, vom Schneeglöckchen bis zur Herbstzeitlosen.  Dabei lernt er (und alle Mitspieler) viele Blumen und auch mancherlei Getier auf einem wunderschönen bunten Plan kennen.

A print-and-play translation of Monopoly into Esperanto.  Adds four new cards to each of Chance and Community Chest.  The money has different sizes for different denominations and is denominated in 'steloj,' or stars.  The property names are changed as well; for example, Pennsylvania Avenue becomes 'Ŝoseo de Zamenhof.'

A game of fast-action finger flickin' fun!  The game materials include a wooden playing field with a goal in each corner, 2 steel marble pucks and 8 rubber "shoes".  Each player or team takes 4 matching color "shoes" and places them on the middle and index finger of each hand. The puck is then "kicked" with the finger shoes.  Players defend their goals by staying outside of their goal lines.  Fouls can be committed and free shots are awarded.  The winner is the first player or team to score 5 goals.

In Port Singapore you are paid by the load.  So your team of grunts better work hard for you. But making money should not be too hard, this is the busiest port in the world.

The Port Singapore board is divided into bays. Next to the board lie the ships that need to be loaded. Each ship has a hold that needs to line up with one of your bays before you can get your grunts to load the goods (barrels).  Along the back of the board is a track on which 4 action cubes sit.  On your turn you pick up one of the cubes, do the action, then slide all the cubes forward putting your cube at the end.  Cubes usually have a cost involved in choosing them, but they get cheaper the farther they move down from the top of the track.  The actions on the faces vary from cube to cube, you may only take the action shown on the top face of the cube when you choose it.

The cubes contain actions like:

    Heave ho.  Pull a ship along the wharf or send full ships off and get new ships to load. Good for annoying players who may be about to load or bringing a ship to your warehouse bay.
    Union meeting.  Shuffle you grunts around the wharf and roll one of the action cubes for a random result. 
    Do business.  This gets goods in your ware house to load or lets you hire grunts to do your loading.
    Load a ship. Move one load per grunt into a ship's hold opposite to your warehouses.
    Take a bribe.  Helps you get things done.
    The Payoff.  Pay one of your opponent's grunts to simply go home for the day.
    Brawl.  Cause a ruckus in the bay next to one of your bays, good for disrupting opponents.


The game ends when the barrels in the supply run low (depending on the number of players).  The player who has made the most money wins.

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Chile, Egypt and CCCP
description:
A trio of expansions that were released by Alban Viard at Essen 2008. The print run was 120.

Chile is a long, narrow map approximately twice the size of a normal expansion and features extremely valuable gold cubes. For 4-6 players.

Egypt is notable for its flood and pyramid mechanics, both of which alter the gameplay in substantial ways. For 3-5 players.

CCCP is a unique new map with altered victory conditions. For 3-6 players.

Pegboard Football is based upon a wooden rectangular board with peg-holes indicating 5-yard increments that are off-set alternating every 5-Yards from one side of the field to the other, representing an entire American Football field, or the Canadian version for one when modified for that, stretching from Goal Line to Goal Line.

Components: 

    Board with peg-holes depicting 5-Yard increments and the GOAL lines at each end of the FIELD.
    2-Pegs of different colours are utilized, to denote the current Player having possession during PLAY. 
    2 d6 required as a minimum, although a 3rd d6 can be used to track DOWNS. 


Alternative methods for creating this:
 1) Magnetized Version: With the appropriate materials at your disposal, then you can affix some reasonable DISPLAY of the Playing Field upon a metallic surface conducive to adhering the necessary components for this. That is in place of constructing it out of anything in which peg-holes were to then be used, for where this is so desired.

2) Paper/Cardstock Materials: From whatever types of this for which you have on hand, and that you can provide the required components needed in order to PLAY, then by all means use such to implement everything and ENJOY!

RUG AMUCK
Ahtnam's Flying Carpet Marketplace has experienced an unexpected magical surge, which has caused the neatly piled flying carpets to run amuck.

The players, representing apprentice carpet riders, attempt to impress Ahtnam by organizing the showroom before doors open for business.

RUG AMUCK is a matching game where players are attempting to match pairs of flying carpets. The player who matches the most pairs will impress Ahtnam and be named the Master Carpet Rider. Just pay close attention to the carpets that all look somewhat alike, and don't forget to watch your back.

Rug Amuck includes:
48 flying carpet tiles (24 pairs)
5 apprentice pawns
1 die
5 player summary cards
1 rules sheet

Rules sheet includes 3 methods of play ranging from friendly to cut throat.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Similar to Mr. Mouth, the Munchlax head rotates in the middle, with the mouth opening and closing as players attempt to launch small plastic pieces of food into it within the allotted time. Players are then scored based on how much food they managed to get into Munchlax's mouth (20 points for a triangle "cheese" piece, 10 for a round "apple" piece, and 5 for a "donut").

This game is no longer in production. 
If replacement pieces are needed, please contact SimplyFun. We'll do our best to replace game pieces as long as they are available in our warehouse.

From original product page:
"Get ready to travel across Antarctica with Riley and his family as you race to rescue five penguins in danger and return them home safely! Packed with informative facts, twists and turns, Penguin Rescue is based on the award-winning book Adventures of Riley: South Pole Penguins."

On a player's turn, two dice with colored faces are thrown.  The player may elect to resolve the dice in either order, which offers one of the minor strategy points in the game.  One side of the die says WILD which allows a card to be drawn which immediately has an effect on the player (move back, move ahead etc).

Be the first to collect a full set of the five colored penguins and return to home to win.

From the publisher's website:

"Based on an ancient Chinese Mahjong game, race to dismantle 136 colorful and uniquely designed tiles by matching identical pairs as fast as you can. The more quickly you make matches, the more points you earn!"

The action is simultaneous and frantic.  Players quickly pull matching tile pairs from among the free outside edge.  (Some tiles are deceptively similar without actually matching!)  The player or team with the most pairs wins.

The 4th expansion for Power Grid, with boards for Korea and China.

The Korean board comes with two separate resource markets (North/South).

The Chinese board has rules for the planned economy in China - power plants come out in ascending order during step 1 and step 2.

Expands:

    Power Grid

"The play on words game"

From the instructions:
Solve the who, what or where questions and race to the center square. At the center square, solve a scrambled question to win the game.

From the back of the box:

During the years since Col. E. A. Drake brought in his first oil well in 1859, the Discovery of Oil has made more men wealthy than any other single endeavor.  Oil, now as then, is just waiting to be discovered.  Yet, only a few men - a special breed of men - seem to solve its Mystery and find it.  What do these men possess that sets them apart from the average person?

They are almost always bold and intelligent... they know Land Values and how to collect and analyze clues.  Their Business Prowess enables them to expand their oil fortune by Investing in other enterprises. They not only own vast empires, but control them with their own decisions. Indeed!  Few people qualify for the extraordinary title of "Oil Tycoon"

CHALLENGE
This game offers you an opportunity to see if you have what it takes to join those elite ranks! You will receive an inheritance to start your own empire.  The oil is out there somewhere just waiting.  The rest is up to you.  With skill and a little luck, you might become the next "Oil Tycoon."

Contents: a board representing a town, 4 coloured pawns, 4 credentials cards, 12 business folders, 20 “biz-bluff” cards, a dice.
Players sell and buy stocks, trying to earn as much as possible from opponents, and of course, to be the wealthier at the end of the game. Pawns movements are decided by the dice, and so are the cards to be drawn, but it is mainly a game of bluff and memory.

Contents: a 8×12 board, 2 holders, 280 letter squares, 20 blank squares, 20 symbol squares, a roulette.

There is no less that seven different games submitted there. 
“Three letters and more” is an adaptation of  a TV game: three letters are set on the board, then each player tries to add a new letter to build a longer word. 
“Crossworlds” looks like scrabble, but unused letters are kept in  holders and the person who stocks 16 letters or more there loses the game.
In “Scrambled Words” each player tries to guess the secret word of his opponent. Anagrams increase scores.

Vaipa Express' Up Front expansion depicting the Waffen SS. This expansion contains a set of Personality Cards for the Waffen SS and rules for their use.

Expands:

    Up Front


Expanded by:

    The Polish Never Quit!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: This is an educational variation on dominoes. Each domino has two three-letter words, with a corresponding picture, such as box, cat, and pig. There are six different ways to play, including rhyming, where the box must match with the fox, or the bug with the rug, etc. The different play objectives allow for greater variation in the learning experience. The game claims to be rated for ages 3 and up.

Power Checkers is an expansion of the traditional game of Checkers. Game play is similar to the original game with the same movement, jumping, and kinging rules. The major difference comes in the expanded board, which allows for more maneuverability and the ability for special "Power" moves.

Players start with their pieces in special territories outside of the regular 8x8 board. To get Power Checker status, the player must get his or her piece all the way through the opponent's home territory to special Power spaces. Once there, the piece may move in any direction, transport to special Power Space Courts outside of the normal play area, and return to their home area.

The game is playable with 2-4 single players or team play.

Campaign (also known as "Buck 20") is a simplified version of Chess, designed to be a good introductory game, easier to teach and to learn.  The board size is reduced to 5x7.  There are no knights or queens, nor are there "en passant" captures.  The medieval piece names have been replaced with modern political terms: candidate, bodyguard, handler, and intern.  Coins are used for playing pieces: one player plays heads and the other plays tails.  For a more tense game, play for keeps.

(From back of box)

SNAPZ is a fast action family game for 2 to 4 players or teams.  It requires the electronic SNAPZ umpire and the category and alphabet cards provided.  The random interaction of these cards produce thousands of possible answers to the simple questions posed, (for example: give a name beginning with the letter for the category shown). But watch out!, at any time the game can increase in speed with the MIME TIME cards - momentarily turning the game into a fast and furious round of charades.  The aim is to get rid of all of your cards by placing them back on the SNAPS unit or to an opposing player.

Serpentine ... a 2, 3, or 4-player abstract strategy family board game.

The board consists of streamers with missing intersections and played with 84 tokens, in 4 colours, that are 2-sided with a straight streamer on one side and a curved streamer on the reverse.
In turn, players place their colour tokens on these spaces.

The goal is to form the longest possible same-colour Serpentine streamer.

The game has 3 levels of play:
Level 1 – in turn, players place one of their tokens on an empty space - and the turn is over. When the board is full or there are no more tokens to be placed, in order to form the longest possible streamer, on their next turn, players rotate or invert one of their own tokens in place.

Level 2 – players place one of their tokens on an empty space. During the same turn, a player may either:
1) Rotate or invert an opponent’s token without breaking a continuous streamer.
2) Move one of their own colour tokens to an empty space, thereby possibly breaking their own streamer.

Level 2 – players take these actions in the following order:
1) Place one of their tokens on an empty space.
2) Move any one of the opponent’s tokens to an adjacent empty space without rotating or inverting it.
3) Move any one of their tokens to an empty space on the board without rotating or inverting it ~ or, rotate or invert one of their own tokens in place.

The player with the longest continuous Serpentine streamer is the winner.

Monopoly clone with Las Vegas gambling elements added.

From the publisher's website:

Competition is tough on Las Vegas Boulevard, and it will take a lot more than luck to build your fortune in this town.  This game has all the action of a traditional property trading game, with an exciting NEW poker twist where players build their fortune interacting with each other playing blackjack, roulette & dice games.  Plus race against each other, to buy and develop your Casino and Resort properties.  Who knows you might even hit the jackpot!  The wealthiest gambler at the end of the game wins!!

Contents : a wooden board with 62 holes, 62 wooden pawns with one side coloured blue, the other side black, 126 letters, two 8 pieces puzzles representing a whale and a sinking tanker  .
The winner is he who has completed first his puzzle. The pawns are played with the same rules that Othello, and when a player surrounds at least 3 pawns, he gets the right to set one piece of his puzzle.

Barbaria represents the history of the west (Europe, N. Africa, Near East) from the fall of the Roman Empire through the Mongol invasion, 406-1250 AD.

Players control several nations (Britannia-like) with differing objectives.

There are two versions using the same map, one using picture-dice combat, 6 turns, 90 minutes, one more detailed and using battle cards (no dice), 11 turns, up to 180 minutes.

In both cases the point scoring is much simplified from Britannia--one 8.5 by 11 inch sheet shows all scoring and appearance, no "nation cards" needed.

MAKIN' MOVIES is a card game about collecting the right actors and money to make a movie.

  You are a low budget movie producer and your goal is to compete against other players to be the first to collect enough money and the right actors to make your movie.  Use your money to protect your actors from other players, or hire their actors!  It's a simple card game with many options and different ways to win. 
 Each turn every player has 3 actions which represent; Holding auditions, casting for roles in your movie and hiring actors from other players.

The game concludes with the winning player naming their movie or giving a quick advertisement trailer for their movie based on the strange collection of actors they have hired.

The FREE Print and Play game contains 44 actor cards, 12 money cards, 8 scripts.

Retail Version contains 50 actors, 12 money cards, and 10 scripts.

Roll-and-Move game where each player controls a superman pawn.

Object of the Game: Be the first player to bring your Villain to Jail and disarm his Secret Weapon at the Crime Lab.

Each player moves around the board uncovering question mark tokens in the hopes of finding the appropriate villain.  Once found, they put the villain into the holder on their pawn and take him to jail.

After depositing the villains in jail, each player must find and disarm their secret weapon.  The first player to disarm their secret weapon (by spinning an even number while the weapon is in their possession) wins.

An easy to play free DTP wargame based on a battle between British and Zulu forces.

A quite unusual subject for a wargame, a battle in 1879 during the war between the British and the Zulus, but featuring some very simple and common mechanisms.

Historically, the Zulus benefited from the imprudence of the British leaders to win the Usuthu battle. Thanks to that imprudence the Zulus forces seized a whole convoy of food and ammunition and, more than that, were informed of British strategic intentions.

The link to this free DTP : http://www.frogofwar.org/?Telechargement:Errata
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Gameplay is composed of two phases.

Initially, the game parts must be physically dug out of a block of plaster.  This takes the theme (digging for buried treasure) and puts players to the act of literally digging their way through to actual treasure.  This is a one-time-event only, although it may take a few hours to fully accomplish.

Next, once the parts have been recovered, players use pieces to move around a pirate's treasure map.  This is a basically simple map, and may only hold the attention of children if they have actually dug the pieces out themselves, and may then appreciate the effort involved.

boardgame: Zahlen Ritter: Spiel um Burgen und Punkte
description:
Zahlen Ritter is a two player game published in Switzerland by Max Giezendanner.
The game contains
24 tiles of each color (blue and red)
2 wooden pawns
2 wooden towers (score markers)
1 board
(the rules are printed on the box)

The players place alternately their tiles face down on the board and start at the score of 50 points. The aim of the game is to grab all tiles of your opponent. You get points when grabbing these and loose points when getting your own tiles. Each tile is indicating the number of points you win or loose.

Each player also has 4 additional tiles with a treasure chest, lady, dragon, and a ghost - worth respectively 20, 10, 10, or 10 points, when getting your opponents' tiles, or losing the same amount when drawing your own tiles.

Tetrade is a three dimensional boardgame on a triangular board with six holes on each side. Players put spheres in alternate turns and try to occupy the four corners of a tetrahedron with the size of 2,3,4,5 or 6 elements. Three spheres of lower level carry one sphere of higher level. A player succeeding in forming a tetrahedron receives a sphere from the hand of the opponent, so that this player is stalemated at the end of the game.

Quarks is played on a 7x7-grid with one player ruling the transparent pieces and the other the solid ones. Both players try to form a column containing all elementary colors with one captured piece at the bottom and two friendly ones in the remaining colors. The first player who forms three of those Quarks wins the game.

Sixon is played on a hexagonal board with five elements on each side, both players trying to reduce the hostile side to less than three. A piece is captured when standing on a cell threatened by three enemy pieces forming a triangle. Pieces may move over the edges of the board when turning their direction in an acute-angled way.

Triade is a positional game played on a hexagonal board with four
elements on each side. Players try to form triangles of any size which   reduces the hostile sides position. Pieces may move overboard when changing their direction.

(from the rulebook, translated from Dutch)
"The Finland game is a boardgame for 2-4 players, where every player can learn about the possibilities of Finland as a holiday destination by doing a tour of Finland."

Depending on the desired game length players get a number of cards corresponding with destinations on the board which have to be reached by moving the player's pawn across the board. There are also additional cards which the players can get by standing on yellow spaces on the board which give players additional destinations to visit or the possibility to e.g. use air travel to move faster. The game ends as soon as a player has no destination cards left in his hand.

Het Finlandspel is a promotional game distributed by travel agencies in the Netherlands in the early 1990s.

This is a simulation of a tennis game. Player throws special dice to serve, then his opponent directly hit the ball back if he is in the right place, or else uses a card to see if he can reach the shot, and so on.

Have fun with your cool friends while playing this action packed internet board game. Answer questions about the things that interest girls the most – fashion, make-up, movies, music, sport and much more. Dare you share your innermost secrets with your friends – if not then pay the penalty! Have fun whilst acting out the craziest of charades or play the word chase game. Land on the internet square and log in to play the game with your friends on the internet. Publish your own trivia questions on the internet for other game players to share.

Online play available.

From the designer's website:

Sinyavino Offensive covers the "crossfire" offensive conducted by the Soviet forces in August, 1942. Crossfire, for it was a daring offensive from two opposite directions focused on a single objective: to free the isolated forces and people of Leningrad before the Germans conducted their own operation to seize the city! The bottleneck area held by German forces was no more than a few miles wide and defended by no more than three divisions. From the East, the Soviets would toss a total of 14 divisions and 100 tanks to smash the Nazi. From the West, across the Neva River, another 2-3 divisions and a tank brigade would conduct an amphibious assault. The battle would last over 30 days and desperate for both sides. At first, Red forces seriously broke through, forcing Hitler to cancel his own offensive to seize Leningrad and divert General Mainstein's forces to save the impending disaster. The battle was also the debut for Germany's new AFV, the Tiger tank!

Contains:
• 11" x 17" map
• 280 counters
• 11 page rules
• 2 page Player aid

This is a funny mixture of Rami and Yams, played with four sets of 32 cards. Players try to improve their hands in order to mark points upon their counting boards. Values give less reward than combinations, but allow access to bonus. After 16 rounds, he who has most points wins.

Reimplemented by:

    Yamy

Bonus game included with the VHS and Laser Disc release of Gargoyles the Movie: The Heroes Awaken (a compilation of the first five episodes).

Components included: game board, "Building" cards, Demona "Wild" cards, Elisa "Badge" cards, spinner (1, 2, 3), and four player tokens (Goliath, Brooklyn, Lexington, Broadway).

Players were instructed to keep the video running after they finished watching the movie.  The video would occasionally flash, and the player whose turn it was would have to follow the orders of the character (Xanatos, Elisa, or Demona) that appeared on screen.
publishers: Buena Vista Home Video (Buena Vista Software)

description:
The Typhon Promotion is a mini-expansion for StarCraft: The Board Game. It consists of a single planet, named Typhon, and the cards and tokens needed to use that planet in the game. The planet is special in that it has only a single connection point, while all the planets in the base game have at least two.

The Typhon Promotion was distributed among the visitors of the 2008 Blizzard Entertainment Worldwide Invitational, as part of a gift-bag containing several Blizzard-related goodies.

Expands:

    StarCraft: The Board Game

ZOMBIE APOCALYPSE! adds bloodthirsty zombies to your New World Disorder: urban skirmishes in a dark future games. Pit swarms of zombies against existing crews, but pay heed--your fallen comrades will rise and turn on you when least expected. Even loot cars and other areas for lost and hidden gear.

Available in PDF format, and as a Printed Set with full-color paper miniatures and cardboard counters.

Expands:

    New World Disorder: urban skirmishes in a dark future

WARCOSM is an easy-to-learn and quick-paced tabletop game that simulates fleet-based space battles using either miniatures or flat counters.

Maneuver the enemy so that you can bring more weapons to bear on them. Find an enemy craft’s weaknesses to maximize the effectiveness of your attacks. Warcosm’s game mechanics encourage such tactics without overwhelming you with endless rules. This permits each player to control six, ten, or even a few dozen ships for scalable battles. Once you get the hang of the rules with the included battle scenarios, use the ship construction rules to build your own fleets. Decide how to arm your ships, the degree and types of hull protection, the power of drive systems, and whether or not to equip them with a number of other subsystems. The fate of the galaxy is in your hands. Command your fleets and lead them to Victory in the Stars.

Available in PDF format, and as a Printed Starter Set with hex-grid sheets, cardboard counters, ready-to-use fleet rosters, and 10-sided die.

Bitwa na Morzu Wiatrów ("Battle of the Sea of Winds") is a nautical wargame set in about XVII century however with no historical scenario. Each player (green and red) owns an identical set of 10 ships varying number of man and guns on the deck. Task of the game is to reach the enemy harbor with the transport ship (without the guns).

Both the wind direction and the battles between ships are resolved using card decks. During the battle each player draws a card which determines the result of gunfire or boarding. There are several damages the ships can receive - the damaged sails, the lost sails, the lost sails and 1 or 2 guns and the fire.

The game was edited in Poland in the mid-eighties.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Bluffing card game about white collar workers fooling around at work. 
One player is designated as the boss and the rest as workers. Each turn, workers choose a card representing their slacking (for points). Then the boss chooses one of those cards to flip over. If you are caught slacking you get no points and may even swap rolls with the boss. If the boss does not choose you, then you will get 1 or 2 points. The winner is he or she that makes 15 points first.

For two to six people, all ages. Experience the thrill of competitive surf action any time, anywhere! Compete for the most points to win. Easy to learn. Great to trade and collect.

Each 104 card deck has six character cards and 12 manoeuvre cards for each player, plus bonus point and penalty cards.

For two to six people, all ages. Experience the thrill of competitive skater action any time, anywhere! Compete for the most points to win. Easy to learn. Great to trade and collect.

Each 104 card deck has six character cards and 12 manoeuvre cards for each player, plus bonus point and penalty cards.

Score Dr. J Basketball Game is an action game that features pictures of Julius Erving on the cover. The game has a shooting device that allows you to try and score baskets from anyplace on the court. The degree of difficulty of the shot determines what value is attached to the basket.

Translated from the publisher's site:

Going down? Collect the most tips by bringing down all your suitcases to the hotel reception. Every player want the lift to stop at floors suitable to them and load their suitcases to the lift.

Move the lift up or down by throwing the dice. If the lift stops at the right floor but is already full of suitcases, a suitcase has to be unloaded.

When you have loaded three of your own suitcases to the lift, you can bring it down to the reception. The player who is the first to bring down his/her 25 suitcases wins the game.

A fun family game full of excitement!

Stymie is one of the games in the "Quickles" set produced by Ashburn Industries in the late 1970s.  Using a modified standard deck - cards numbered 1-12 in four suits, plus six wild cards - players add to a central sum, trying to avoid pushing it over 100.  Players then score points, with the objective of the players also being to avoid going over 100 (or reaching 0, from an initial score of 50).

Hexad is one of the games in the "Quickles" set produced by Ashburn Industries in the late 1970s.  Using a modified standard deck - cards numbered 1-12 in four suits, plus six wild cards - players attempt to reach the total given in the center first by using addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, and the numbers in their hands.  An advanced variant is offered wherein 2s can be used for square roots, 3s for cube roots, and so on.

Travel Bingo comes with ten dice featuring objects that might be seen while driving, such as animals, vehicles, and buildings. Players roll the dice into the included tray, and then try to spot the objects for points. Before rolling, one die is set aside to customize the game to the players' location. (For example, the orange die has farm-related objects, so it is set aside if the players are in a town or city.)

The game is designed for a single player or multiple players working cooperatively, but includes rules for playing competitively with multiple sets.
family: Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

description:
Stock Market game simulating the real stock market.

Parts:

One game board
6 markers - in the shape of small ticker tape machines
1 pair of dice
16 Good Tip cards
16 Bad Tip cards
16 Good News cards
16 Bad News cards
32 stock certificates for made up companies
Paper money totaling $1 billion

Winning the Game:  To be the first player to gain outright and complete ownership of all the certificates in 4 different companies.

Contents: a 20×20 board, 9 squared orange pawns and 9 squared yellow pawns, a rectangular platform 4×2.

The platform is set in the centre of the board, and the players try to access it. Pawns can move aside diagonally or jump over others. Some moves depend of the number of pawns upon the platform, which can also be moved. He who have put 5 pawns upon the platform wins the game.

boardgame: Trollball "this is not a fair game"
description:
Based on the original Trollball game from Greg Stafford, this new game has been published (with Greg Stafford's authorization) as a fund raiser for the Tentacles convention(May 2008).

This is a violent fantasy sport simulation. The game comes with a 120x60 cm color board, color counters, card board miniatures, player statistics sheets and a 20-page rule booklet.

The game is simple and fast and best played with 5 players, one being the giant referee.

100 copies of the game were produced, with 90 to be sold, numbered 1 to 90 and 10 for people who were involved in the game (drawer, play testers, Greg Stafford, etc.).

Two player abstract strategy boardgame with very simple set of rules, but hours of fun gaming. Easy to learn.

Black and white players take turns to place tiles according three rules and tries to make other player into the position, which is impossible to continue.

It can be played on a standard 8x8 chessboard, but smaller and bigger boards are possible. The size of the board will affect the difficulty and length of the game and also the winning tactics and strategies.

boardgame: New Parlor Game: The Path to Matrimony
description:
Game board has decoration of a maze with arrows which either allow players to move backward or forward.  Considering the age of the game, a spinner was most likely used for movement.  The center of the board one lands on "The Alter."

W.J. Morgan & Co. operated in Cleveland Ohio from 1871-1908.  It was a lithography firm that produced trade cards and advertising posters for sewing machines, stoves, medicines and petroleum.

boardgame: Go On or The Run for the Stake
description: Game board is in a circular pattern where a person goes from circle to circle trying to get to the "Stake" first.  Board does not have a maker's mark, but it is similar to McLoughlin Brothers boards.  The date on it is copyright Jan. 1884.  Wonderful printing lithographed colors.

Game where players strive to become the President of a business.  Pitfalls along the way include a late train, missed street car, arrested, late for work etc.  Four corners of the board have a Home, Hotel, Hospital and Court.

Pieces include:

gameboard
4 different colored metal figures
game spinner showing how many spaces to move

The games purpose is to promote good work habits and characteristics which lead to success

boardgame: The Game of Bamboozle or the Enchanted Isle
description: This was one of the first large folding board games produced by Milton Bradley.  Game board has a wonderfully lithographed fantasy land where one takes paths to get to the Enchanted Isle.  The center of the board has two castle, woods, bears, a tent, a windmill and a church.  The paths weave along the outside and into the center.  The paths along the outside represent water and has pictures of a steamboat, canoeist, serpent, and whale.  The four corners of board are one city scene, one house scene, one mermaid and one lighthouse. This particular version, with a plain box (likely considered "deluxe" at that time) is later than the first 1876 version, which probably featured a more decorative printed image on the box cover. This version probably dates from around 1900.

Sandbox hill is a miniatures wargame that uses plastic army men as soldiers and die-cast metal cars as tanks and can be played in about any location.

The game requires you supply:

    Soldiers: at least 60 army men preferably in two colors
    Tanks: at least 6 die cast metal cars (hot-wheels, matchbox, etc)
    Artillery: several hundred large rubber bands
    Mines: a hand full of quarters (or equivalent coins)
    The Injurator: One large pair of scissors
    The Mauler: One large hammer
    The Moverator: A piece of string twelve inches long.
    The Rangefinder: A standard twelve inch ruler.
    The Iron Dice: One or two six-sided dice.
    Eye Preserver: Two pairs of safety goggles


In the game players take turns activating 5 soldiers or tanks, most can move and shoot on turn but there are a variety of different troop types with some special abilities modifying this. Dexterity based rubber band flicks are used for ranged combat and die rolling for close combat. Damage is represented by either a die roll that tells what part of the infantry figure to cut off with The Injurator (large scissor) or for tanks a blow with The Mauler (large hammer)

Tamerlane Chess is a strategic board game related to chess and shatranj.  It was developed in Persia and was favored by the 14th century Turco-Mongol conqueror Timur the Lame (also known as "Tamerlane"), who thought regular (queen's) chess was not complex enough.

The board consists of a 10 by 11 unchecked pattern along with extra squares are called citadels.  This version of chess has multiple types of pawn, and each one promotes itself based on the type of pawn, i.e. Pawn of Pils promotes to Pils (Elephant).

The playing pieces include the king, the general, the vizir, the giraffe, the picket, the knight, the rook, the elephant, the camel, the war engine, and pawns.

Players complete sentences by placing jigsaw pieces with words on them from their hand.

The shape of the 124 puzzle pieces enforces correct grammar in the sentence, but the sentences can be very silly indeed such as "The furry clock ate the big princess." or "A blue crocodile jumped over the school."

All the pieces except the 40 nouns are dealt to players. The nouns are reserved in a stock for players to select one when they want/need to. A players hand goes down by one, except when they play a noun.

The winner is the one to get rid of all their words first. The strategy is therefore to block other players and force them to select nouns because they cannot play any other valid words.

Along the way a lot of fun is had by placing unexpected nouns in the sentences. Subtitled "grammar skills practice for the first three years of school", various classroom activities are also possible and included in the note to parents and teachers that accompanies the rules.

The BACW book adds rules for multi-day battles and entrenchments, and adds a point system for constructing ad hoc balanced ACW battles.

A strategic game (heavily based on GDW "House Divided" rules) is included, allowing players to play a campaign game if they so desire.

boardgame: DEFCON ONE: The Cold War Goes Hot
description:
The year is 1959 and world has descended into madness. A Capitalist Empire, lead from Washington D.C., is locked in mortal conflict with the Communist Bloc, controlled by an iron fisted Kremlin. For how eagerly the two sides have rattled their sabers, neither power is truly prepared for total war in the nuclear age. Tanks and missile cruisers, jet fighters and Kalashnikovs, roll off assembly lines and straight into one of a dozen charred wastelands strewn across the globe. Each side prays for a quick victory, as neither can sustain the horrors of the nuclear age for long.

DEFCON 1 is a diceless wargame meant to loosely simulate a nuclear war between the US and the Soviet Union. The game supposes that neither nation’s economy is prepared for nuclear conflict. Production represents the sole random chance element in the game. Each turn, players draw random units according to the size of their economy, and strategically deploy them across the globe, each side struggling to achieve victory by taking their opponent’s homeland before their economy suffers total collapse.

A simple dexterity game - the play area consists of a disc suspended from a pole. Players take turns to roll the colour dice and choose which type of weight to add to the disc. Larger weights score more points. The game continues until the disc tips over.

Can you get all your balloons safely into the stratosphere?  But watch out for falling hailstones!

Each player has 4 balloons and tries to move all of them from the bottom to the top row of the Sky Grid.  The first player to do so wins the game.  If a balloon hits a hailstone then the balloon is burst and returns to the bottom row.  Players may also burst other players balloons by squashing it between their own balloons and/or a hailstone.  Balloons can jump diagonally over balloons of the same colour (counts as one move) all the way up the Sky Grid thus being an efficient way of using the action-points allocated each turn.

To begin a game, the 10 hailstones are lined up in the stratosphere (one in each square).  Each player places their pawn on the corresponding 'Big Balloon' space on the Flight Path on the outside of the board.  Then players place their balloons on any squares on the bottom of the Sky Grid (each player places one balloon in turn until all balloons are placed).  Several balloons can occupy the same square for the first 3 rows (green area).  Beyond row 3 (in the blue area) - only one balloon to a square.

On a player's turn they roll a 10-sided dice and move their pawn around the Flight Path.  When the pawn lands on or passes certain squares it gives that player a number of action-points (1-4) in which to move their balloons in an upwards direction (vertical or diagonal).  The action-points may be split among the player's balloons as they see fit.  When a player's pawn lands on a 'Storm Clouds' square on the Flight Path the player must move any hailstone of their choice down one square.  If a player's pawn lands on a 'Wind Clouds' square the player must move any one of their balloons one square sideways (if that is impossible - their lowest balloon is deemed burst, and falls back to the ground.)

Players can choose to stop their pawn on the Storm/Wind Clouds squares even if the number thrown on the dice could take the pawn past.

When a player's pawn passes or lands on a Kite/Cloud square in the corner of the Flight Path the player also throws the dice to determine which of the 10 hailstones moves down one square.

The game ends when one player has moved all their balloons to the top of the Sky Grid or when a hailstone hits the ground (in which case each balloon carries a score for the height it has reached, the highest total score then wins the game).  For a quicker game, players can agree that the first balloon to reach the stratosphere ends the game - then the highest total score wins.

A cooperative print-and-play children's game where one to four players work together to try to complete as many deliveries as possible before nightfall.

The board depicts 16 stations connected by rail lines.  On the delivery stations (beige), one of each of the eight destination tiles are placed randomly, face up.  On each of the pick-up stations (green), two passenger/freight tiles are placed randomly, face down.  Players roll a die to move (and sometimes advance the sun along its path in the sky).  As trains arrive at a pick-up station, players reveal the goods/passengers.  Sometimes, the player will load them onto their cars (limited number of cars on each train).  Sometimes, they will leave them for other players to pick up.  A player can even take a passenger part-way and leave them for another train to complete the job later.

The idea is to work together to see how many jobs will be completed before the game ends.

The title translates to Quick (tooth) Brush.

Two horseshoe shaped wood pieces carry 20 wooden teeth pieces, zero to three wood rings are laid over the teeth, the players walk around this "mouth" with their pieces, wooden tooth brushes. One die shows how far they may go, another die specifies what action to perform: brush away one ring, two rings, do nothing or advance a bacterium to the teeth.
If the bacterium reaches the teeth before the players have cleaned all of them, the players lose.

Components include a map of the continent of Demara and the surrounding seas, a deck of Action Cards tailored to the theme, and unit counters.

This game is a module for the Warp Empires system, the core rules are required for play.

"Based on the animated film Wizards by Ralph Bakshi. One player is the good Wizard Avatar. The other player is his evil brother Blackwolf. Both battle for world domination. On Avatar’s side is Magic, Elven Races, and Faerie Folk. Blackwolf's armies are filled with Mutants, Demons, and Ancient Technologies."

The Blackwolf player must destroy all opposing units to win, otherwise the Avatar player wins.

Components include a map of the land of Halcionia, two decks of Action Cards (one for each side), and sets of unit counters.

This game is a module for the Warp Empires system, the core rules are required for play.

A game of fantasy conflict and conquest between the forces of good and evil, based on the Lord of the Rings.

One player controls the Free Peoples and the other player controls the forces of Sauron. The winner is the player who controls more territories after twenty turns. Either side wins automatically if all opposing units are destroyed.

Components required to play include a map of Middle Earth, a deck of Action Cards tailored to the theme, and sets of unit counters.

This game is a module for the Warp Empires system, the core rules are required for play.

A game of fantasy conquest involving armies of conflicting alignments. Inspired by the Mystara campaign setting of Dungeons and Dragons.

Players must choose to play as Lawful, Neutral, or Chaotic empires. The winner is the side that destroys all opposing units.

Components required to play include a map divided into the individual territories of the fourteen nations of Mystara, a deck of Action Cards tailored to the theme, and unit counters.

This game is a module for the Warp Empires system, the core rules are required for play.

A game of fantasy conflict and conquest between the Sorcerer Kings who rule the City States of Athas. Inspired by the Dark Sun campaign setting of Dungeons and Dragons. The object of the game is to destroy all opposing Sorcerer Kings.

Components required to play include a map showing the territories of Athas, a deck of Action Cards tailored to the theme, and unit counters.

This game is a module for the Warp Empires system, the core rules are required for play.
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

description:
A game of fantasy conflict and conquest involving Warhammer armies.

The players take control of one of eleven Armies: Dwarf, Ork, Empire, Skaven, Dark Elf, High Elf, Goblin, Chaos Dwarf, Wood Elf, Bretonnian, or Undead. Each army has a unique set of units. The object of the game is to eliminate all opposing units.

Components include an area movement map, a deck of Action Cards tailored to the theme, and sets of unit counters.

This game is a module for the Warp Empires system, the core rules are required for play.

A game of science fiction conflict and conquest involving Warhammer 40,000 (Third Edition) armies.

The players take control of one of four armies: Ork, Imperial, Eldar, or Tyranid. Each army has a unique set of units. The object of the game is to capture over 80% of the planets on the map.

Components include an area movement map of space, a deck of Action Cards tailored to the theme, and sets of unit counters.

This game is a module for the Warp Empires system, the core rules are required for play.

"The Doctor and Sabbath are both looking for the scattered remnants of the Elementals, the Lords of Time who never existed. Chief among these artifacts is a black sun, the EYE."

"He who controls the EYE controls the new dynasty of Time Lords for the universe. But while the Eighth Doctor has looked without for the Eye, he has not tried to first look WITHIN."

"The TARDIS was once infinite, but now only a small part of its interior is accessible. And Fitz is hearing strange sounds behind the unusual walls that have partitioned the TARDIS' interior. And now a door to the mysterious cloister room has opened. Could the EYE have been in their all the while? But one challenge stands in the Doctor's way of
exploring the cloister room and unveiling its secrets: SABBATH!"

"One Player plays the Doctor. His goal is to move from the CONSOLE ROOM to the CLOISTER ROOM. The other plays Sabbath. 
His goal is to prevent the Doctor from reaching the cloister room."

"The eighth doctor has lost his memory. He has a whole new band of enemies. One player plays Sabbath. The other is the good doctor."

"There are three decks: One for the doctor, one for Sabbath  and an 'elemental' artifact deck."

The winner is the player who obtains the most elemental artifact cards.

boardgame: Doctor Who: The Keys of Chronos
description:
"The old Time Lords are gone, and many races are competing to take their place as the Lords of Time. In order to forge a new Gallifrey and a new order of the Lords of Time, the Eighth Doctor must scour time and space to recover the six keys of Chronos. This will insure the new Gallifrey protection from its rivals, and bring order to all time and space."

At the start of the game, each player is dealt six Mission Cards. The six cards identify the six keys that the player is searching for. The first player to obtain all six keys named on his Mission Cards wins the game.

boardgame: Doctor Who: The Lords of Time
description:
"Gallifrey has been removed from space and time, but nature abhors a vacuum. It is up to the inheritors of their legacy to forge a new Gallifrey and a new order of the Lords of Time."

"Up to four players may play this game. There are four sides: The Eighth Doctor from Earth, Sabbath from the Moon, and Empress Miranda from the Throne World of the Universal Empire, and the cyborg Silver who has escaped exile from the Planet A2756."

The first player to reach the New Gallifrey space in the center of the board by exact count wins the game.

boardgame: Doctor Who: Remnant of the Daleks
description:
"The last of the Daleks have invaded the TARDIS Console Room. And it’s up to the Eighth Doctor, Romana and K9 to stop them from taking over the TARDIS. Whoever controls the TARDIS controls all of Causality."

Each player has three units. The Doctor player has the Eighth Doctor, Romana, and K9. The Dalek player has three Daleks.

The conflict takes place in four TARDIS areas: the Console Room, Library, Clock Garden, and Altar Room. Each player has a card deck which is used to randomly determine movement between the four areas. The cards are also used to resolve combat.

The object of the game is to defeat all opposing units.

SMART stands for Simple Miniatures Adventures Rules and Tactics.

"These miniature rules may be used for fantasy or medieval miniature war gaming, but can easily be adapted to any genre. These rules were inspired from Little Armies by H. G. Wells and Kreigspiel."

The rules are described as being suitable for any scale from 100:1 to 1:1.

A skirmish level science-fiction miniatures game based on the short story "A Prayer Answered Just in Time" published in Gateway magazine.

"Nestled in the six extra dimensions of the Calabi-Yau Manifold (which is a plane higher than the four spatial dimensions of Hyperspace) every event in space in time meets with the Interface. The Interface of Time is a great ten dimensional construct which consists of archways that lead to any world at any point in that planet’s history. Humans can only perceive the first three spatial dimensions of the Interface. Thus it allegorically appears to them as a contemplative cloister with seemingly endless arcades leading forth from a monumental courtyard."

"In this game one player plays the mysterious Virtue and his human friends. The other player plays the infamous Vice and his company of evil time travelers." The object of the game is to destroy the opponent's four warriors.

A science fiction card game based on the Lensman stories. It is an expansion designed to be used with the Rebel Space rules.

The game pits the Galactic Patrol of the First Galaxy (Milky Way) against the Baskonian Pirates of the Second Galaxy (Andromeda). The Galactic Patrol player has a deck of 65 cards and the Baskonian player has a deck of 63 cards. The object of the game is to reduce the opponent's deck to zero cards.

Expands:

    Rebel Space

A science fiction card game of the Rebels versus the Imperials. Despite the generic card descriptions, it is clear that this game is inspired by the original Star Wars saga. Thus you have cards like the Smuggler, Converted Freighter, and Alien Companion, as well as the Young Protagonist, Wise Mentor, Princess, and Robot Sidekick.

Each side starts with a unique deck of 65 cards. The object of the game is to reduce the opponent's deck to zero cards.

Expanded by:

    Lensman

A game of battling giant robots (Meks) and the pilots who ride in them (Mercs).

The rules describe nine pre-designed Meks. Players can also design their own Meks using the chassis, weapon, and other Mek systems specifications given in the rules. Mek pilots are individually rated for experience and skills.

The game is played on a 3x3 grid. Each square is marked with a terrain type as specified by scenario. The game comes with six scenarios.

boardgame: Warmeister 2: The Battle of Plentymore Field
description:
"This is an expansion for Warmeister, not a game in itself. You need Warmeister to use these rules."

This expansion adds approximately 180 cards to the Warmeister deck. There are new cards for Attacks, Castles, Armies, Holdings, and Events. The rules also define three new card traits.

"A fantasy warfare card game for 2-6 players"

The object of the game is to capture the Territory Tokens of the other players, representing conquest of their lands.

The game uses a custom deck of 130 cards. Each player starts with five Territory Tokens. Tokens may be won and lost as a result of battles battles out through card play.

The players represent various elements of the U.S. Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) seeking to assassinate Fidel Castro back in the fifties and sixties.

The game uses a custom deck of 57 cards. There are seven types of cards: Method, Conspiracy, Location, Save, Event, Propaganda, and Spy.

The players attempt to play melds of cards of the same type. Once you have three melds in play (one each for Method, Conspiracy, and Location), you may make an Assassination Attempt die roll to see if you kill Castro. If the roll is not successful, you lose some or all of your melds and the game continues.

Save and Event cards can be used to require an opponent to discard a meld. Propaganda cards allow a player to draw additional cards. Spy cards allow you to look at an opponent's cards and steal one.

"Winning: Be the first player to Assassinate Castro. If Castro is not killed after the players have gone through the deck twice then Castro survives and all the players lose."
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Grand battles between fantasy forces based on the Warcraft game system. Control an army of Humans, Orcs, Night Elves, or the Undead. Enhance your army with Mercenaries and Magic Items. The object of the game is to establish control over half the territories on the continent or eliminate all of the other players.

This game is a module for the Warp Empires system, the core rules are required for play.

A simple game of individual combat with firearms, in the style of first-person shooter computer games.

"The objective of the game is to blow the bajesus out of your enemies. You take turns moving and shooting.  Each turn, you get two actions. Actions are usually either moving, shooting, aiming or reloading. Each of these usually cost one action."

"The Evil Mad Scientist Doctor Herr Guffin has left his assistants (Igor, Igor, Igor and lest we forget, Igor) in charge of his experiments while he goes to a convention on 'Evil Inventions and the Applications in Modern Society'."

"The experiment in particular is a machine he designed to breed a better rat (because the kids these days had gone and invented a better mousetrap). It is a mixture of mechanical and electronic feeder/torturing device."

"The object of the player (called Igor) is to have his favourite rat the biggest by the time the master returns."

"This involves making sure that toggles tilt the pipes in the machine such that the food pellets go to the player's rat - and avoiding/rotating the shocking motor such that the other rats are shocked (which causes them to lose food pellets, probably through vomiting)."

"There are event cards as well (things like Cardiac Arrest - fattest rat gets a chronic heart attack and dies)."

"A simulation of the War in Heaven from John Milton's Paradise Lost. In it, the forces of Satan fight the forces of God in a battle for control of Heaven before the Son defeats them and they are cast out of Heaven and into Hell."

The game is based on Books V and VI of Paradise Lost. The Satan player must destroy a certain number of Heaven's forces to win the game. The Force of Good player wins by preventing Satan from achieving his victory conditions.

"Generic Rules for Combat in any scale or era."

A universal and generic wargaming system for recreating battles from all eras, and from fiction, using a hex map, counters, and dice.

Unit stats are included for World War I, World War II, Napoleonic campaigns, Feudal Japan, the Middle Ages, and science fiction spacecraft.

In addition, there are army lists for Middle Earth conflicts as depicted in Lord of the Rings and Star Wars spaceship battles.

A generic rules set for recreating ancient battles on a variety of scales.

"The basic concept is to be able to have: Relatively quick battles with 20-30 units to a side. A minimum of record keeping. Easy set up. A very simple rules-system, easily learned, and easily modified."

Contains Army Lists for the Greeks, Persians, Macedonians, Indians, Carthaginians, Romans, Gauls, Celts, and Huns.

"Imagine if Orville and Wilbur Wright did not successfully fly their powered airplane in 1903, but instead ten years later. In such an alternate history there would be no effective combat airplanes. Commitments in military research and industrial production might have instead, focused on the Zeppelin as a weapon carrier. This game provides a tactical simulation of such Zeppelin warfare."

"Skirmish is a game of warriors on the battleground fighting in the midst of chaos. Bodies lie everywhere, and there are enough of the armies remaining that none can retreat. Each warrior is without a side to aid them. All other players in the game are their enemy. The last warrior standing is the winner."

"Inspired by Atlas Games' Lunch Money."

The game is available on the author's website here-
http://www.curufea.com/games/skirmish/

"Shapeshifting wizards duel to the death, fight over magical artifacts or seek certain objectives."

"Every player starts with one Wizard. Each wizard is a Human with a pool of 50 Mana. Mana is used to cast spells, change shape, and activate special abilities of the shape the wizard is currently using. Mana can only be recovered in Human form, once per turn."

The game includes three scenarios:

"Dueling Wizards - Two age old rivals meet for a final showdown. "

"Wizard War - Rival kingdoms at war send their court mages to take care of the competition before invasion."

"The Key of Soloman - An artifact of power has been unearthed, the magical emanations in the ether have attracted treasure seeking wizards."

"Concept: A primarily two player game. One player is the owner of the mansion, the other is the thief. The owner spends resources to set up the mansion, and the thief tries to steal the treasured item. The behaviour of the guards in the mansion are mostly automated responses. If more than two players - then the additional players are additional thieves."

"Setting: The world of Thief is a steampunk setting. There are gas, coal, steam and some electrical powered machines. There is also some magic."

"Inspired by the various Thief games from Looking Glass Studios that have been part of my favourite computer games of all time."

"Much like the Incan Gold game (Diamant), it's a game of pushing your luck and knowing when to betray the other players to get away with as much gold as possible. Every turn a player decides whether to play a card to continue journeying into the caves, or to retreat with their current gains. The chances of dying and not making any money increases as players move further into the caves."

"The difference between this game and the original is that it uses normal playing cards, and there are more choices for the players involved."

The rules are available on the author's website

"Card Game for 2 or more players. Players are 17th century alchemists, competing for the creation of the Philosophers Stone."

The game uses a custom deck containing three types of cards: Elements, Procedures, and Substances. Elements and Procedures must be combined to create Substances, which in turn can be combined with Elements, Procedures, or other Substances to create more complex Substances.

"The first player who creates the 'Resurrected Lapis Philosophorum' wins."

"This is a modifiable dungeon-crawling game for 2-4 players."

One player takes on the role of the Dungeon Master. The other players each control a Hero who is exploring the dungeon. The game uses three to five custom card decks: a DM Deck (60 cards), a Dungeon Deck (30 cards), and a Hero Deck for each Hero player (60 cards.)

The Dungeon Deck consists of cards specifying rooms and passages which are used to randomly create the dungeon. A Hero Deck consists of cards for various actions and treasures. The DM Deck consists primarily of cards representing monsters and traps.

The Hero players win if the Objective room card is located and resolved. The DM player wins if all of the Heroes are killed before they resolve the Objective room.

"This game simulates the growth of ancient empires. Each player leads a young empire and controls its growth through its development. The units used to represent people are populations and monetary units are measured in gold points."

"Use a piece of hex paper and color equal numbers of squares green for grassland and yellow for desert. Each player chooses a starting grassland hex and puts a city and 3 population points on that hex. No two starting hexes may be closer than three hexes from each other."

The players build their empires by constructing cities, roads, temples, walls, marketplaces, and libraries. There are also three types of military forces available to attack opposing empires: Soldiers, Swordsmen, and Horsemen. The game also covers concepts such as disasters, technological advances, taxes, and population migration.

"Victory occurs when, at the end of his turn, a player has 20 victory points or has destroyed or captured all enemy cities, armies and populations." Victory Points are scored for cities, temples, and technological advances.

"Solo Crusade is a simple set of rules to play a solo adventure with your favourite RPG. The underlying idea is very simple: one or more pools of 'tiles' are used to represent the map, the encounters, the events and in general every possible obstacle/help during your quest."

"Using a pool of tiles rather than simply roll some dices to create the adventure has a some major of advantages"

"• Visual representation of your travel: the tiles played on the table are much more " satisfactory" than a never-ending sequence of dice rolls."

"• Better control of the random factor: you can have tiles that can be drawn only once, tiles that are replaced when played."

This set of rules allows you to play out solo RPG adventures on the tabletop, similar to games such as DeathMaze: Heroic Adventure in the Corridors of Doom and the Metagaming MicroQuests series. The rules include tables and instructions for four sample adventures: The Wilderness, Hobgoblin Forest, Hunt for the Tribe, and the Black Pit.

An antique spin-and-move board game themed around a hurdle race.

The components include a spinner, four different colored wooden pawns, and the box (which has the board mounted inside the bottom and the rules printed inside the lid.) In the race around the board, the players must clear three hurdles, two water jumps, and avoid a marauding bull.

Publication date is approximate.


     Lid text includes "It Is Great Sport/Milton Bradley Co. Springfield, Mass." 
     Milton Bradley inhouse art depicting Buster and Tige, Foxy Grandpa, one of Katzenjammer Kids on lid.
     Inside board has animals and humans w/recognizable characters of Buster, Katzenjammer, Foxy Grandpa, Happy Hooligan and Gloomy Gus, Black character likely representing Pore Lil' Mose and four human or animal characters inspired by Swinnerton. 
     Instructions on inside lid w/© 1905. 
     3.25 x 3.25" spinner

"Card game for 2 players. Setting: Turn of the Century United States. One player takes the role of Edison. The other player takes the role of Tesla."

Each round the players start by building their hands. The players alternate selecting one Research card from a set turned face up on the table. The players then to play melds of cards. A meld becomes a part of the player's Victory Pile.

"Victory: The player to score the most total Invention Points worth of Primary Cards in their victory pile at the end of 4 Rounds wins."

A card game of warring Ninja clans in Feudal Japan. The object of the game is to be the last surviving clan, or to have the highest ranking Ninja when there are no cards left in the deck.

The game uses a custom card deck which is composed of two types of cards - Ninja Cards and Event Cards. A player must recruit Ninjas and then send them on missions against opposing villages and Ninjas.

"Board game for 2-4+ players. Players are caravan merchants carrying goods back and Forth between China and Arabia."

"The Board: The board consists of a track 9 spaces long. The leftmost space is Arabia. The rightmost space is China. Arabia and China are referred to as destination spaces. The 7 intervening spaces are called road spaces."

"The Deck: Players share a common deck. There four main types of cards: Imports (Goods from Arabia), Exports (Goods from China), Road (Terrain along the Silk Road), Special (Obstacles and Opportunities)"

"Money in the game is counted in units of coins. The first player to accumulate 25 coins is the winner."

"Each player takes the role of a Soviet Sniper during WWII."

"Each player has 3 stats: Shoot, Hunt, and Evade. Shoot is needed to kill Targets. Hunt is needed to find Targets. Evade is used to survive Danger. Players start with a score of 5 in each Stat."

"Players share a common deck. The Deck has 4 types of Cards: Targets, Danger, Aid, and Nothing cards."

"The Game ends when all but one player has been killed, or the Deck has been completely used up. The player with the most Kill Stars at the end of the game wins."

"Card & Record Keeping Game for 2-4+ players. Each player is a European Imperial Power circa 1500-1750."

The players seek to conquer 40 territories spread across five regions: the Caribbean, South America, Central America, North America, and Canada. Rather than relying on a map board, each territory is represented by a card in the Exploration Deck.

Players can gain control of territories through colonization or military conquest. The capability to do so depends on successful production and trade efforts, which provide the resources required to raise armies, build ships, and hire workers. The players must also be ready to cope with random events, such as piracy, storms, and revolutions.

"Card Game for 2 players. WII with Wonder Weapons and the Occult as the main determinants of victory."

"One players is the Allies. The other is the Germans."

"Players have scores in 9 Stats. These stats are increased/decreased during play. Players begin with scores in each stat as listed on the Starting Stat Chart."

The nine Stats are: Intelligence, Attack, Occult, Research, Propaganda, Morale, Force, Economy, and Defense. The Allied Player and the German Player start the game with specified Stat scores.

"You win if you reduce your opponent to zero Morale or zero Force."

A diceless game of global conquest played using the board, pieces, and cards from Risk. Territories are captured through card play. The winner is the player having the most Victory Tokens after all six continents have been filled with units.

A free online rules set available at http://www.angelfire.com/games2/warpspawn/ER.html

"Card Game for 2 players. Theme: The Satsuma Rebellion in Japan in 1877. The Modern Imperial Army destroyed the last of the Samurai. One player is the Samurai. The other player is the Imperial Army."

The game uses two custom decks of cards, the Samurai Deck and the Imperial Deck. Each deck contains eight types of cards, each ranked with a Strength value: Force, Reserves, Leader, Attack, Defense, Morale, Weapons, and Escape.

"The game ends when the Samurai player has accumulated 9 Casualty Markers. At that point, the last of the Samurai have been wiped out. Victory is a function of how many casualties the Samurai were able to inflict on the Imperials."
family: History: Boshin War & Meiji Era Rebellions

description:
"Card game for 2 players. Theme: New Zealand Maori Wars 1840-1872. One player is the Pakehas (White Men). The other player is the Native Maori."

The game uses a set of 19 Land Tokens and two custom decks of cards - a Maori Deck and a Pakehas Deck. There are nine different types of cards, each named for aspects of the conflict (Leaders, Tactics, Weapons, Politics, Morale, Defenses, etc.) Each card is ranked with a Strength value from 2 to 5.

Each hand the players battle for the possession of a single Land Token. Tokens are won by playing a set of cards having a higher total Strength value than those played by the opponent. The first player to acquire ten Land Tokens wins the game.

"Area Control game for 2-6 players. Early Earth Primitive Microbial Life Forms Compete for Territory." Each player represents one of six different Microbial Kingdoms which have special attributes or abilities.

The game is played on a map composed of 64 Territories. Each space on the board has a set resource value from 0 to 5. The resource value governs the maximum number of Biomass Tokens that may occupy the Territory at the end of each turn.

"The Game ends when all territories are filled up to their 
Max population levels." Victory Points are then awarded based on factors such as the number of Territories a player controls and the number of Biomass Tokens each player has in play. The player with the most Victory Points wins the game.

"Strategy Card and Board Game for 2 to 6+ players. It is the Warm Permian-Triassic period and primitive animals are just starting to colonize the landmass of Pangaea."

Every player controls one of ten "Basic Taxons" representing a group of prehistoric animals. Each Taxon has a special ability.

The object of the game is to have the most Victory Points after every Territory on the board is occupied at least one Animal Token. Victory Points are awarded at the end of the game based on the number of Animal Tokens a player has on the board, as well as the number of Territories occupied exclusively by the player.

"Card, Bidding, and Stock Ownership Game for 2-6+ players."

"Each player is a large Multinational Corporation. 
Players invest in different World Powers."

"World Powers represent Nations and whole Regions.  
There are 9 different World Powers players can invest in."

"The basic monetary unit is the Treasury Token. The player with the most Treasury Tokens at the end of the game is the winner."

"Bidding game for 3-5 players. Each player is an Imperialist Power in the late Nineteenth Century trying to get a piece of China." Each player plays one of five Imperialist Powers: Britain, France, Germany, Russia, and Japan. Each country has a unique special bonus.

The game is played using two shared card decks - the Territory Deck and the Imperial Deck. The game ends when there are no cards left in the Territory Deck. The winner is the player having the most Victory Points, as determined by the combined point total of the Territory Cards in the player's Victory Pile.

"The game is a simulation of the hardships faced by the first English Colonists to the New World in the 1600s. Each player controls their own colony, modeled after Jamestown, the Plymouth Pilgrims, and the Boston Puritans." The game consists of six card decks: a Leader Deck, Site Deck, Action Deck, Build Deck, Food Deck, and Hardships Deck.

"The game lasts 5 years. This represents the first 5 years of each players colony. Each Season is a turn, so the game runs for 20 turns."

"Victory: The player with the most Victory Points (VP) at the end of the game wins." Victory Points are scored for the number of Colonist Tokens, Land Purchase Tokens, and Buildings a player possesses at the end of the game.

boardgame: Ancient Battles Deluxe Expansion Kit 1: Elephants at War
description:
From the far reaches in Africa and Asia, ancient armies would mount a howdah on the back of a mighty pachyderm and transform it into a War Elephant! How will Alexander meet this new threat when invading India and meeting King Porus at the Hydaspes river? Why was Hannibal so careful to bring elephants across the treacherous passes of the Alps in the winter? Whose elephants will reign supreme when they clash at Raphia?
Find out in the first Expansion Pack for Ancient Battles Deluxe: Elephants at War.
family: History: Wars of Alexander the Great

description: "The Spanish Empire: 15-18th Centuries. Each player is a Noble Family in Spain, trying to serve the Crown by arranging the loans and manpower to fight the Wars and Discover, conquer, and exploit new lands in the name of Empire."

Description extrapolated from TheBigGameHunter.com:

ZULU BLOW GUN GAME (1925, Zulu Game Co., Battle Creek, Michigan)  comes from a category of games no longer available: the "projectile" game. Safety requirements have virtually eliminated all sharp, pointed projectiles from games (other than DARTS), and although the packaging on the ZULU BLOW GUN GAME states "harmless," the game's bamboo dart, when made airborne from the blowgun, could pierce many things, including body parts. Object was to score points by hitting different valued targets using the blowgun and bamboo darts.
Childs game which features animal sounds which must be matched to the animal pictures in order to score points.

"War in Ancient China." The players take control of one of the following ancient armies: Chou, Ch'in, Han, Hsiung-Nu, Korean, or Central Asian. The object of the game is to capture all territories and eliminate all opposing units.

Components include an area movement map of China, a deck of Action Cards, and a set of unit counters.

This game is a module for the Warp Empires system, the core rules are required for play.

Roll and move game from the 1960's. More info on how to play the game to follow. Game features a playing board, 4 plastic men and 2 dice.

The game is about two clan chieftains fighting for territory.

The winning player is the Chieftain whose eventual strength of territories, Clansmen and dues, defeats all other players by surviving until all the other Chieftains lose all of their territories.

It was designed and published by Marketplan for the House of Seagram, to promote Seagram's 100 Pipers brand of Scotch whisky.

boardgame: Defenders of the Earth Giant Card Game
description: Children's card game featuring the actions of Superheroes as they try to defend Earth from invading aliens. The object of the game is to collect 10 or more Hero points before collecting 10 villian points. There are Power Of Evil cards that may be used to cancel out the point value of any one Hero Card diplayed by an opponent. If you accumulate 10 Villian Points you are immediately out of the game.

boardgame: The Chisholm Trail from Texas to Dodge City
description: The object of the game is for each player to get his herd of 1,000 head of Texas Longhorn cattle up the "The Chisholm Trail" to Dodge City, Kansas. The game comes with a game board, a spinner, 4 metal longhorn tokens, 6 blue cards, 6 red cards, a pad of tally sheets, & a rule book

You try to circle Wide Track Drive in Pontiac, Michigan in this game created to promote the host city of Superbowl XVI. The game was created by I&I Game Inventors of Pontiac, MI. and is copyrighted - 1981. The object of the game is to be the first player to circle Wide Track Drive, the main road in Pontiac,  while being sidetracked along the journey by drawing instruction cards directing you to various venues of the city such as the mall, Church's lumber, Lupe's restaurant, Pontiac police station, Pontiac High school, the Silverdome etc.

"Generic rule set for a series of empire building/conquest games."

Games designed to be played using the Warp Empires system are called Warp Empires modules. Each module describes the units and other components needed to play the module.

Warp Empires modules are generally played on a map composed of irregular areas or territories. Counters are used to represent units, items, and control markers.

Card play is central to the system. One or more Action Decks are used in each module. Action Cards are played to move units or stacks. In addition, some Action Cards provide benefits in combat.

Victory conditions generally involve controlling a certain number of territories or destroying all opposing forces, as specified in the individual Warp Empires module.

The following games use the Warp Empires system:


    Babylon 5 Empires
    Conquest of Demara
    Dark Sun Empires
    Doctor Who Empires
    Doom Trooper Empires



    Empires of Mystara
    Europe at War
    Federation
    Land War in Asia
    Lord of the Abyss



    Middle Earth Empires
    Scortch
    Space 1889 Empires
    Star Wars Campaign
