description:
In this all luck cricket game the pitch is represented by a  6 by 6 grid on which the fielder places his nine men. The bowler uses a spinner to determine the outcome of the bowl. If it's a hit further spins determine which square the ball lands in. Should there be a fielder in that square the batsman is out, otherwise the lower of the two numbers on the spinner is the number of runs scored. After each over the fielder can reposition his men.
Play continues until all eleven batsmen have been dismissed. Then the other team (player) have an innings. The winner is the player who scores most runs.

Muumien sanasirkus is a box with two seperate games. Both games are use letter tiles and the aim is to create words with the tiles. The theme of the game is based on the Muumi characters.

Game is a Finnish Game of the Year 2008 finalist.

Game similar to Action D-Day, with a chessboard that simulates the battleground where (with 4 mountains to be positioned to liking) you are moved the miniatures through the game of the card and draughts of die.

The Savings Book is part of the Zooloretto-News No. 1.
With this little addtition a player can get additional money during the game.

It is also part of the Rio Grande Games Exp. Pack #2 with several mini expansions.

Expands: 
Zooloretto

Also available as part of the Zooloretto: Goodie-Box.

Home Page & Newsletter:

     www.zooloretto.com 


(German, english, dutch, hungarian, spanish, swedish)

"Mystic Master -- an adventure for the mind"

Presented as a game, Mystic Master is pretty unusual for its time. Coming in on the fringe of the D&D explosion in the early 1980s. It's games like this that made some people take the magic use in D&D way too seriously.

From the tube:

"Mystic Master is a unique and enjoyable adventure designed by a research psychologist to test, challenge and help develop the fantastic powers of the mind. Join forces with Merlin in mastering mystical mental powers of ESP. It's fun and easy to play. The game comes complete with:

"A long-lasting, attractive, vinyl game board, 18 specially designed mystic image cards and easy-to-follow multiple game instructions."

No designer is actually credited in the rules or anywhere else. It also comes with two gold plastic tokens.

Movement is literally governed by guessing (erm, divining) what card(s) is being held up, is closest to the top of the deck, etc.

boardgame: A Castle for All Seasons: Winter Cards
description:
This expansion to A Castle for All Seasons was released at the fair SPIEL in October, 2008.  The expansion contains 6 new winter cards which are shuffled with the existing winter cards. As only 6 cards are needed for playing the game the cards may replace the cards of the original game.

The cards were given as exclusives to everyone who bought the game A Castle for All Seasons directly at the booth of Eggert-Spiele (German edition) or The Game Master (Dutch edition).

The cards are:

     Hungersnot (Famine)
     Sonderzoll (Extra Duty)
     Raubritter (Robber Baron)
     Feuerstelle (Fireplace)
     Strassenbau (Road Works)
     Bettler (Beggar)

A twist on classic checkers. On each turn players spin to see if they either move, or open and close trap doors. If your checker is on one of the trap doors – look out it might fall through. Adds cunning and luck so that kids and parents have equal chances of winning.

boardgame: Walt Disney's Pirates of the Caribbean
description:
Pirate Ship shooting game based on Disney's "Pirates of the Caribbean" ride.

Game includes two pirate ships with cardboard sails and hull and two plastic cannons for each.  You load and shoot black plastic cannonballs trying to knock down the three plastic pirate figures on each others ship.  When the third pirate figure is knocked down, a trigger releases the "Pirates Treasure" and you win the game.

Also included are 5 Buoy Markers and 6 Course Cards for determining movement of your ship before shooting.

Players use their color of cards to make five in a row. Each player starts out with either the red or black half of the deck and attempt to make five in a row either vertically, horizontally or diagonally with their color. A card may be played on top of an opposing card with the same rank.

Speculation is played using two standard decks of cards divided into a stack for each suit, with six cards removed from each stack. Each suit represents a commodity.

Each round one card from each stack is turned over to give the price for that commodity. All players may buy or sell the four commodities. If a face card is revealed for a commodity all holdings in it are wiped out. The wealthiest player after a set number of rounds wins the game.

Capitalist Canasta is played with two standard decks of cards and a supply of money. Players attempt to make small melds and cash them in to get enough money to make larger melds which are worth more money.

On a turn a player draws a card and then may make melds, but they have to be paid for: 5-10 cost face value but J-A cost significantly more. Deuces are wild. Cards may be added to existing melds by paying for them.

Melds may be sold to the bank. Melds may also be raided with 3s and raids may defended against with 4s.

Tortoise & Hare is a racing game that uses two decks of standard cards. The goal is to reach 100 points.

On a turn a player turns over one card and then may turn over more, but if a face card is drawn they bust.

When a player reaches 100 points they may stop or continue. When a player does stop the other players have one more turn to try to beat them.

The winner gets antes and penalties from a pot, but the rules do not state to what end; presumably one plays a number of rounds and then the player with the most chips from these pots wins the overall game.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: The Tortoise and the Hare

description: Missouri Rummy is a rummy/poker variation where poker hands are used to win various pools of chips. For instance, a pool will made for club flush, diamond flush, heart flush, full house, etc. and it is up to the players to determine when to play their hand for that pool and cash-out. The longer the pool remains, the more chips it can potentially pay out.

Released in Essen 2008, Fund Manager is a game about the stock market. Player with the most asset, cash and portfolio, in the end wins. The game comes with two decks of cards, one sheet for stock prices, paper money and a board.

Fund Manager can be played with either three or four players. Depending on the number of players each have either three or four action chits numbered from one to three. Players use this chits to move one single pawn around the game board. When the pawn lands in a space that action is then executed. These actions spaces include buying/ selling stocks, drawing random events from the deck of cards, rolling dice to see which companies rise or fall and collecting cash at the completion of one round. When round is completed the chits are turned over and are ready to be used again. Game is played for eight round and takes about one hour to complete.

description:
Players advance their frog by taking frogs from the bag and placing them on the board. But some frogs don't like others - so frogs also have to be at the right color. By placing frog of the wrong color You find Yourself right where You started. But You can always stop risking and say that You'll stay right where You got with the last frog.

At Essen '08 there also was this poster: "These frogs are not edible!"

(from the rules) There are millions of different animal species world-wide, which have not even yet been studied. Likewise there are many animals living in the oceans, at the coast and pols which most people have already seen, but sometimes cannot put a name to or say much about.

GOAL OF THE GAME

Simultaneously, all of the players try to collect as many animals as possible with a certain characteristic. Whoever collects the most animals wins.

This game should be played in darkness. Every player gets a certain number of fluorescent cards into his hand and has to pass one of these to his left every round. Whenever one player collects a set of four equal pictures he calls the end of the round.

Points are scored for ending the round and for ghosts which can be either positive or negative, depending on the ghosts' face which can not be recognized in darkness.

Lariat is played on the surface of a cylinder with one open end and one closed end.  As the name suggests, players must form a "lariat", a group of stones which either includes the center cell or surrounds the center cell, and which includes a cell on the lip of the cylinder.

Lariat rule sheet:
  http://www.marksteeregames.com/Lariat_rules.pdf

From the publisher: The object of the game is to assemble a "complete" plant or animal cell.

This is an educational and fun game simulating the workings of a cell.  Each player can complete two actions per turn, which consist of cellular functions like respiration as well as drawing "cell event" cards that simulate important environmental changes.  Each player must accumulate as many points as possible, the play is complete when a player has assembled the parts of a complete cell.  The player with the most points wins.

From the back of the packaging:
Brainwash is an exciting memory game featuring characters from the hit movie Igor, and the Dreaded Brainwashed game play card!

IGOR Brainwash Game includes a total of 51 cards: 2 brainwashed cards, 1 Old Bone card and 24 pairs of cards that show characters from the movie.

3 sets of rules are included with the game. All are based on the classic Memory game but two of them offer simple twists.

Game titles are:  
Classic Memory, Brainwash Memory Game and Old Bone.

Queen of Hearts is an Alice in Wonderland themed Hearts variant.  It is played with a 36 card, non-standard deck (spades, diamonds, and clubs suits contain cards A-9, hearts consists of A-8,Q).  The 36 card deck allows the full deck to be used when playing with 2, 3, or 4 players.
A simple game where people try to build a path by laying tiles on the table. These tiles can be acquired by rolling a die. You can also use the die result to obtain action cards or move the pawn.

In Apokalypse each player gets a hidden card that gives him his game goal. There are two different cards, good and evil. The Good players can win all together, but only one Evil player can win.

On your turn you can play a population card, if you put it on the Good side you must give one of the cards from your hand to another player. If you put it on the Evil side you can steal a card from another player. You can also play cards to protect your population from the Apokalypse riders.

The game ends after the 4th Apokalypse rider.

The Good players win when all player's populations are more Good than Evil, and the Good players have scored more points than the evil players. The Evil player wins when the populations are more Evil than Good, and the Evil players have more points than the Good players, but only the evil player with the most points win.

boardgame: ZORBOW: A Galactic Adventure With Numbers
description:
ZORBOW was designed to teach and reinforce the math skills in addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.

Players place number and problem cards in the correct patterns as they attempt to solve math problems.

Can be used in the classroom as a team game.

Contains 1080 cards, plastic card holders, and rules.

Includes a Sign Language version.

(c)1989 Abby Educational Products, Atlanta GA

Educational matching and race game based on the characters from the movie Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa

From the box:
"No Animal Left Behind!
Take off on a journey of fun with the cast of Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa.  Players move their characters across the game board on a trip to Africa.  Any time they match a card with the space they are on, they choose a Colorforms "Stick-On".  The story board fills up as the matches are made.  The more matches the better the final story.  The player to reach Africa with the most matches wins, but everyone wins by creating a unique story every time!

The game teaches and enhances memorization, matching, and comparison skills.  It also encourages young childrens' imaginations as they build stories around their favorite characters.

Contents:
Game board
15 picture cards
4 character game pieces
Spinner
Story Board
Sheet of 15 Colorforms Stick-ons
Instructions

This is a dice and chips game, where players roll two dice and attempt to place a majority of their chips in one of 12 spaces in four different colors for an area majority win.

Components are two six side dice, gameboard, chips, instructions, and game tin.

Naturquartett Wasser is a small collection of games by Haba. The cards are Wind- & Wetter-Karten; sturdy cards that you can clean when taken outside.

Components:

36 Cards
1 Wooden frog
12 Wassersteine (beads)
Rules

With this small package of components, you can play different games: Quartett (A Game of Authors), a Quartett variant, a Quartett variant for 2 players, a Charades / pantomime game. The rules also suggest to go outside with the cards and search for all the animals pictured on the cards. The number on the card shows how many points you earn for finding that animal. The more rare the animal, the more points you get.

Also in the rules: a few tips on other outdoor activities. Measure the depth and speed of water, make your own watermill, etc.

This two to six player game of the rise and decline of historical empires could be described as a cross between History of the World and Vinci with a little Britannia thrown in.  While the participants play historical empires, the game does not recreate any particular history, hence allowing players to fully exercise strategic judgment.

Players control empires on a map of Eurasia, ranging from the earliest history to the Mongols.  As time passes empires decline and finally disappear and new ones appear, but in the meantime receive new armies depending on terrain type and empire type (steppe, desert, agricultural).  Empires have historical point centers, and also have historically-based special characteristics such as leaders, religious zeal, light cavalry, and cataphracts.

There are 80 empires, with optional rules for use of 30 event cards.

Depending on the number of players, their familiarity with the game, and the victory target selected, the game lasts 60-140 minutes.

A bounch of adventurers explore the cursed kingdom of Telendar, in search of a key to close the Gates of Evil, end the curse and win! They fight monsters with brute force and magic, gaining treasures and artifacts, they gain followers, visit strange places and they trade with each other or in the four burghs of the map.
But there is a ticking clock: the King of the realm is slowly dying. Each turn, an event card is turned over. This could lead to the King lose lives, and when the King dies all the players lose.
Each game is different, with a random 7x7 tiles map, tons of monsters and encounter in different combinations, and you need to use very different strategies every time like improving fighting skills, becoming a very powerful magician, exploring the land for money, tempting fate and, last but not least, trading watermelons.

Can be played solitaire.

description: A game about hunting animals in Africa. Players roll dice and move their meeples. On many spots on the gameboard they can take a chance and shoot at elephants, lions, ...  For each "killed" animal they get counters worth from 1 point for a jackal up to 80 points for an elephant. Animals, which give many points are harder to kill than animals which give less points. All in all there are 29 different animals. During some actions the hunters also can die. When the surviving hunters are back in the native village, the one with the most points wins.

The Grain Elevator is a campaign game for Advanced Squad Leader that appeared in the Critical Hit 1997 Special. It includes a two-page map of the famous grain elevator in Stalingrad, which was the scene of fierce fighting in September 1942.  The campaign game uses Critical Hit's Platoon Leader system for linking several days of combat (in this case four CG scenarios) into one game.

Monopoly - Perry Rhodan was a freebie given away at Essen 08.

It consists of a map printed on glossy paper and cards to be punched out. The rules appear to be standard Monopoly rules, but themed on the SF series Perry Rhodan, with sets of planets rather than hotels. Free Parking and Jail still make an appearance!

The game is printed in German but could easily be played by anyone familiar with ordinary Monopoly.

Simple word game. Comes with 165 Plastic pieces with letters on.Aim of the game is to build and change the major word in the middle. If there is eg.the word STORE and you would have an Y you could change it to STORY and the E would be a point for you.

Players pass one card at a time to the player on their left, until one player completes a set of 4 matching. When the set is played on the table, all players grab for stick from the table. one player will not get a stick. The first player gets the letter of LUCKIE, the player without a stick gets the next letter of STRIKE. When the someone completes LUCKIE, you score +1 for each letter of LUCKIE, -1 for each letter of STRIKE, highest score wins.

The Cat in the Sack is a very simple dexterity game. It consists of a large plastic base in the shape of a blue sack. Inside the sack is a large white cover for the sack on which is mounted a large plastic cat head. The cover is mounted to the sack with a large spring (reminiscent of a jack-in-the-box). Along the edge of the "sack" are 25 clips that hold the cover in place.

To play the game, you push the spring loaded cover down into place, securing it in place with the clips. The game begins and each player takes a turn removing one of the clips, trying not to let the cat out of the sack. The player who removes a clip and causes the cat to spring out of the sack, loses the game.

Counting quickly and snatching the correct number of cards from the middle of the table - that's almost all there is to it, but this is easier said than done. Since while checking the motifs and colours, there could suddenly be more or less.

The game contains 14 number cards that are laid out on the table. Each number card has three numbers on it. For each round, four motif or search cards are turned up. Players look at the search cards, which may show colours or symbols, then count the number of matching colours and symbols on the motif cards. When they think they've got the number right, they take one number card from the middle of the table.

The numbers are checked, and everyone who has a correct number cards scores a point. The number cards go back into the middle of the table and the next round begins.

If the cards turned up are only search cards, then the result is zero - and there are number cards with zero on them, so those are the ones people try to grab. If only motif cards are turned up, another set of cards is turned up.

There is a variation for 2 to 4 children aged 6 and up, with fewer number cards, placed the other way up so that there is only one number showing. In this version, the search cards and motif cards are separated and only one search card is turned up each round.

This game uses an 33⅓ LP recorded by New York Yankees announcer Mel Allen to determine the outcomes of baseball plays. Each side of the LP has seven tracks, for use in different game situations. Each track has four interleaved grooves announcing the resulting play, leading to different outcomes based on which groove happens to catch the phonograph needle.  The back of the LP sleeve itself is used as a game board.

WWF Superstars Card Game was sold in double packs featuring two WWF wrestlers.  Each wrestler is represented by a 30-card deck, although the card distribution is identical for each deck.

Players draw a hand of four cards and take turns either playing or discarding a card and then drawing a new one.  Players try to have the highest Takedown card in the center stack.  Break] cards restart the stack.  If a player is unable to play a card, they must discard.  If your opponent discards, you can then play a Pin card, which can only be defeated by an Illegal Hold.  If you are able to play two [i]Pin[/i] cards and then a Referee, you gain 3 points, and a new round begins.  Else, if both players' decks run out, the last player to play a card gains 1 point.  The first player to 5 points wins.

Out of the box, the game is for 2 players.  However, a second set allows for 4-player tag team matches.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Fair Shake -- "The fun filled fast moving game of luck for two to six players"

(c)1972 Leisure Life

"The pace is quick, the action exciting in this fascinating game of dice and cards. Fair shake deals you a handful of fun."

Players are dealt five cards from a special deck and then roll five dice in an attempt to match the die roll with cards they hold. Players can make a second roll without revealing their card combinations.

Players score based on how quickly they discard matching sets.

Includes cards, dice, six plastic card holders, and rules printed on an illustrated storage insert.

Trail 98: A Historical Card Game of the Klondike Gold Rush.

Half-a-dozen cards make up the playing board,  and players use their cards (claims, goods, strike, hit, bonus, passage, provisions) to make their way from Seattle to Dawson and to lay down as many claims as possible. Player with the most claims wins. But you can put a hit card like Avalanche, to slow players down and it's likely you're run into a storm, sandbar, log jam or some other hit cards along the way.

From the instruction book: "Welcome to the Klondike gold Rush of 1898! This card game takes players up the scenic Inside Passage from Seattle through historic Skagway and along the rugged Chilkoot Trail to Dawson, where they work the gold fields. along the way, players encounter difficulties of the trail, as well as some bonuses..."

Lookee is the "Search-A-Word Game", for 2 or more players, that can be played by those of all ages. The game consists of a plastic tile board and 100 various tiles each with a letter and point assigned to it on each side. The game has five different variations of play. In Game One: The grid begins empty except for the word LOOKEE spelled out in the center. Using at least one letter on the board each player must take turns making their own word (with the 7 tiles they began with) using at least one pre-existing tile.Each turn must be finished within 30 seconds. These words could be spelled forward, backward, horizontally, vertically or diagonally. Each player scores points for all the letters on their new word. After each turn, the player who went replenishes back to seven tiles. The player with the most points after the grid is filled is the winner. Game Two: First player to reach 100 points wins. In this game, the entire tile board is filled with letters at random. The first player tries to locate a word in any direction, and receives the added points for that word. The tiles used are then flipped over creating a new board every turn. The next player goes and so on. Only 30 seconds per turn. Game Three: Same as game two except additional option. Look for letter groupings that can be rearranged to form words. E.G. T,C,R,U,K can be TRUCK. First to 100 points wins. Game Four: Play solitaire by setting up the board as in Game One. Score as many points as possible in a given time period. Game Five: By using another game board, a duplicates version could be played by using identical letters and setting a time limit.

Reed's Game of Base Ball is a dexterity game where players use little wooden bats to whack a dowel seated in a groove on the edge of the game board. The space where the dowel comes to rest indicates which Baseball play has occurred.

From the box. A Family Get Together Game.  A competitive fast moving game of skill & Strategy with a unique playing board.  There are three ways to line up your marbles and hundreds of ways to play and win.  Strategically designed blocking panels.  Mislead your opponent ... attack and counter attack.  Think one or two moves ahead and hope your opponent spoil your game plan.

Klapse - German for "Nuthouse" - is a Monopoly-like roll-and-move game with a strong satirical approach. There are two "circles" - the "locked ward" and the "open ward" - for the pawns to walk around and step on "event"-spaces of different kinds: New diagnosis, several events ...

The players try to get their pawns from the "locked" into the "open" ward and from there to freedom stepping on a "discharge" space. In the beginning, players draw a "diagnosis", which reduces their possibilities in walking forward.

There are many satirical cards and texts on the board, plus in the "active" variant (the full game) players can discard their "diagnosis" cards, when acting out the symptoms ...

A game without depth or strategic approach, but a pure mock and fun game about psychiatrists and looney bins - mainly made for people working in that area (the publisher is specialized in critical literature about psychiatry).

Jerusalem is  a German Game about a sight-seeing tour in modern Jerusalem. The Players have the tasks to make photos of places of interest. The plans for each tour are hidden from the other players. Special events are handled by event-cards. Additionally the players have to bargain for special souvenirs. The player who was able to make all photos and buy all souvenirs first wins.

A game of skill, intrigue and fun(chess/draughts level). Based on 3,000 years of folklore, customs and superstitions of the spring festival of fertility. Players pit their wits against each other to move their own 15 pieces, which can be moved in varying ways, across the maroon and white board while outmaneuvering, blocking and capturing the pieces of their opponents with the aim of scoring the most points.

A western-themed card game in which players attempt to play card combinations to draw guns (2,2 ; 3,5,7, ; 4,5, ; 3,8) and shoot the cowboys (kings) from other players hands, while trying to shack up their own cowboys with harlots (queens) along the way for extra points.  At the end of the game, players gain or lose points for the sets of cards (gangs, posses, harems, orphanages, etc.) that they've managed to collect, during play.

Home Page: http://www.wyomingcowboy.org/

From the box:

The starting signal for the big horse race is audible and the horses start to run. After a while you notice an exciting neck and neck race on the racetrack. The lead changes all the time. Place your cards in a clever way so that your horse wins in the end. An exciting horse race for 2-4 players where the horses are moved by number cards.

Gameplay description:

There are two different ways to play the game:

1. Each player plays a card on his turn and moves his horse.

2. Each player plays a hidden card and all the cards are revealed at the same time. The lowest cards and the highest cards are discarded, and the other players move their horses. Two special cards (gallop and walk) can change this procedure.

After ten rounds (each player has ten cards), the player with the most lap token wins.

Spartacus is a solo game. The player represents Crassus attempting to defeat Spartacus and his Slave Army. His objective in this is to acquire personal prestige, and so it is important to the player that Spartacus is destroyed or driven from Italy before Pompey or Lucullus arrive with their veteran armies, who will presumably make short work of the slaves.

From Wikipedia

Hardtack was the first U.S. published American Civil War rules set and had an early following. However, by the end of the decade, it had been supplanted by Scott Bowden's Stars and Bars and John Hill's Johnny Reb 3 and is rarely played at modern wargaming conventions. However, it is considered as groundbreaking, and ushered an era of Civil War miniature wargaming in the USA.

Introduction by Gary Gygax
Painting Guide by Don Lowry

Guidon Games Product # WM103

Preface:

This comprehensive set of rules is designed to take a person who has never played miniatures before thru several increasing stages of realism to the point where he can create for himself the kind of Civil War game which is most suited to his own personality.  When you have read and played everything this booklet contains, you will be prepared to design a Civil War game which has all the simplicity necessary for a great game.  The first version described is called the TRAINING GAME because it is designed to teach the basic mechanics of recreating Civil War battles with miniatures soldiers.  When it can be played without referring back to the rules, learn the more realistic BASIC GAME.

Gangster: Der Clou is a small expansion for Gangster. The game is released at Essen 2008 in a limited edition of 999. Components: 4 black gangsters and 1 Lady Rosina Ginger tile.

The four gangsters give the players more influence on the course of the game and conspirator Lady Rosina Ginger can be called for support.

Release: Essen 2008
Price: € 4,90
Players travel around the board answering questions about the bible. The first player to reach the finish line is the winner.

Based on the Italian Comic Book / TV show "Monster Allergy". Each player has his/her own deck of cards, choosing 30 out of the available packs. There are cards in five colours, but players are only allowed to choose cards of three colours, so the influence on the cards played in the game by the other player might be limited. Also, several icons and numbers, plus some descriptive texts showing different values, powers and abilities of the cards.

The rounds are "days", in which in a strict order powers, friends, monsters and so on are placed onto the board. This has two personal zones - one for each player - and a neutral zone, in which the monsters are placed and attacked/captured in the main phase of each round. Aim of the game is to capture monsters with the total value of "10" through activating the power cards, but the other player can protect monsters with his/her cards, losing these powers for capturing doing so.

After the "attack / capture" phase, there is a "night phase", in which several monsters` activities are executed. Some more twists are interlaced in the system of the cards, influencing each other and offering some tactical decisions.

In the starter set there are two decks with 30 cards each - the total of available cards is 111.

Packed in a nice "Bombo" monster figure as storage for cards, player mats and rulebook.
Advance to GO! and play the Detroit edition of the world's most famous board game.  Or so the back of the box proclaims!  This version highlights the Motor City!

Letter is an unusual word game.  As with many word games, there are letter tiles which are laid on a grid in order to form words.  What is unusual about Letter is that the letter tiles are different sizes: 1×1, 1×2, 1×3, 2×1, 2×2, 2×3, 3×2, and 3×3 (because orientation matters).  Words are identified by chaining along orthogonally adjacent sides of the letters.  As a result, the board can quickly come to look like quite a jumbled mess of letters with varied sizes, but this provides opportunities for lateral thinking in playing that next tile.  Each play initiates a Boggle-style word search, but with deference to the active player.

Debuted in Essen 2008.

boardgame: Lawyers, Guns & Money: an Ambush Alley Scenario Pack
description:
In conjunction with Ambush Alley Games, Rebel Minis is releasing another great scenario: Lawyers, Guns & Money!

The scenarios in Lawyers, Guns & Money form a campaign of three interlocking scenarios which focus on a VIP protection detail performed by the fictional Private Military Contractor (PMC), Aegis Group in the equally fictitious city of Dawala

Lawyers, Guns & Money contains an exclusive mini-campaign written by the Ambush Alley Team!

This is a pdf booklet that can also be purchased as an attachment to "Lawyers, Guns & Money! Battle Box", that contains the 15mm miniatures to play the scenarios.

This is a set of nine scenarios for Advanced Squad Leader.  The scenarios all include German units from the 7th SS Gebirgs Division "Prinz Eugen".  This division consisted of mountain troops made up of ethnic Germans from Croatia, Serbia, Hungary and Romania.  In six of the scenarios the Germans are pitted against the Yugoslavia national army or Yugoslav partisans.   One scenario has elements of the "Prinz Eugen" division fighting the Italians, while the final two has Bulgarians as the opponents.

These scenarios tend to be a mix of medium and large numbers of troops.  Here is a summary of each scenario as provided by Lone Canuck Publishing:


    PE-1 Operation Weiss- Boards 39,40, and 5-turns; a FT & DC toting Prinz Eugen SS-Engineer Platoon supported by 2 x 37L AT-Guns and a pair H38 French Tanks rush across a bridge to seize a Partisan Outpost.  Meanwhile, a Prinz Eugen SS-Company having crossed the river downstream earlier hotfoots it to reinforce the Engineers just as a Yugoslav Company arrive.


 

    PE-2 Operation Schwarz- Boards 3, 50, and 7½-turns; a Prinz Eugen SS-Battalion consisting of 18 squads packing a ½-dozen Axis Minor SWs, supported by a platoon of H38 French Tanks and 81mm Battalion Mortars OBA launches an assault to capture a village from a Yugoslav Company reinforced by HMG and a platoon of 76mm World War One artillery pieces.


 

    PE-3 Shot Like Rabbits- Boards 9, HOB II, and 8½-turns; a full scale assault by a Prinz Eugen SS-Battalion consisting of 24 squads supported by HMGs, 81mm mortars, and 105mm OBA on an desolate Italian mountain stronghold made up of concrete bunkers, wire, and trenches.  The Italian force consisting of 15 squads and numerous crews man, 2 x 81mm mortars and a pair of 150mm artillery pieces.


 

    PE-4 Operation Kugelblitz- Boards 39, 52, and 6½-turns; a blizzard had struck dumping a heavy snowfall across central Yugoslavia, a Prinz Eugen SS-Company consisting of 10 squads and supported by a HMG, a 37mm AT-Gun, and an 81mm mortar has set up a blocking position along a forested ridge line.  A Yugoslavian battalion- consisting of 30 squads and guided by Partisans, look to force a pass over the same ridge line. 


 

    PE-5 Operation Scheensturm- Boards 34, 36 and 8½-turns; a sequel to Operation Kugelblitiz, has a Prinz Eugen SS-Company consisting of 10½ squads supported by HMG and 81mm Mortars are spread thinly along a deep forested stream in central Yugoslavia.  There they are assaulted by a Yugoslavian battalion consisting of 28 squads guided by Partisans looking to break out. 


 

    PE-6 Better to Sweat than Bleed- Boards 3, 40, and 7-turns; a Prinz Eugen SS-Heavy Weapons Platoon consisting of a HMG, a 37mm AT-Gun, a pair of Light mortars and a medium mortar struggles into position to provide fire support on the Partisans guarding the far bank as a Prinz Eugen SS-Company consisting of 9 squads and commanded by a 9-2 race forward to seize a bridge and the village before Yugoslavian reinforcements. 


 

    PE-7 Operation Draufgänger- Boards 14, HOB II, and 9-turns; a Prinz Eugen SS-Company consisting of a dozen squads and supported by a pair of HMG and 37mm AT-Guns fans out to capture and a Yugoslavian airfield defended by a pair of 37mm AA-Guns and the over watching mountain fortification manned by a dozen Partisan squads armed with medium machine guns and light mortars. 


 

    PE-8 Former Allies- Boards 23, 49, and 7-turns, the fortunes of war have change and the Bulgarians, now former allies are advancing into Yugoslavia.  A depleted Prinz Eugen SS-Company consisting of 7½ squads reinforced by platoon of engineers and supported by platoon StuG IIIs, have been give the task of getting rid of an unwanted bridgehead.  All that stands in their way is a Bulgarian battalion consisting of 15 squads armed with German SWs and supported by a pair of 50mm AT-Guns. 


 

    PE-9 No Sight for Sore Eyes- Boards 4, 17, and 5½-turns; the sequel to Former Allies, as the Bulgarians in battalion strength (15 squads) armed with German SWs and supported a platoon of Panzer IVs, now launches out of the bridgehead and streams southwards en masse.  A Prinz Eugen SS-Company consisting of 7 squads quickly sets up a delaying position to slow the on rushing Bulgarians.  During the night they receive much need support from a pair Luftwaffe 88mm AA-Guns formerly of the oilfields at Ploesti.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: The object of 'Full On World Soccer (i.e. football) is to score more goals than your opponent. To do this, you move your eleven players (coloured discs representing one of 32 countries) around the board to control the ball. Each turn the player in possession of the ball rolls three six-sided dice (including one directional die) and moves the ball accordingly. The aim is to move the ball into the goal or to another one of the players on the same team to retain possession. The game ends at the end of the allotted time. Two 30 minute halves are suggested in the rules, however any length game could be played.

From the publisher:

Officially licensed Monty Python game of buying and selling property in King Arthur's Britain while hunting for the Holy Grail.

You, too, can quest for the Holy Grail, buy hovels, build castles, enjoy a spanking, and even make it to Scene 24! Charge excessive groats for hanging out at your Castle of Aaarrrggghhh in this great game based on Monty Python and the Holy Grail.

A trivia game for children. Collect one card from each category by answering correctly, then move to the goal square in the middle of the board to win.

Similar to Trivial Pursuit.

The goal is to get four of one kind of card. The active player asks another player of his/her choice for a card, and if there is a match he receives it and gets to ask again. If not, the turn goes over to the next player in line.

This game is similar to another Swedish cardgame called "Finns i sjön" (Go fish)  which is played with an ordinary deck of cards.

The goal of the game is to score points by associating from categories such as "famous love-couples" etc. You either get point from associating the same as your competitors, or uniquely, depending on how you roll the dice.

The Ski Sprung Spiel (in English: Skijump Game) is trying to simulate a ski jump competition. After team selection each player will roll dice for selecting the highest position on the jump tower. Therefore all seven dice are rolled. After each roll at least one die has to be set aside face up. When all dice have been allocated the jumper is placed on the tower according to the rolled total result which is equivalent to a certain jump distance in meters. After all players are set up one player puts one of his jumpers for the jump. Then card play takes over. The effect of cards is to add or subtract jumping length from the a fore determined jumping length (the dice roll) or to move the jumper left and right on the hill simulating wind. Other players play cards for this jumper which are countered by the jumping player. This goes in turn i.e. player A plays card jumping player plays card, player B plays card jumping player plays card and so on until each non-jumping player had the chance to play a card. If the jumper is moved too much to the left or right or too long or too short of the board it is counted as a fall. Finally the jumping player plays a card for the landing again adding or subtracting metres. The final result will be noted down. The player which ski jumper has the farthest jump wins the game.

description:
a resource management game for 2 to 4 players, using the Decktet

The Lord High Chancellor has retired, but the King has not yet named a successor. As one of the King's Chancellors, you are hoping to move up to the first seat. To do this, you need to impress His Majesty and outdo the other Chancellors. Fail and you'll be a back bencher for the rest of your career. Of course, things could be worse than being a mere Chancellor. If things go too badly, the King might decide to fire all the old advisers.

description:
The "Ö-Deck" brings 24 new cards for Agricola. Theme of this expansion is Austria (Österreich, that's why it is called Ö-Deck). language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
A fun little game from the early 1970s drug culture. The goal of the game is to make money buying and selling "kilos" (of what is left to your imagination).

Simple roll and move mechanics. Start with $1000. Cross the border to buy kilos from various sources (Edwardo, Renaldo, Jose, ...) then attempt to cross the  border and sell your recent purchase in various U.S. cities (from Tucson to New York).

If you're carrying kilos you can be busted by various U.S. government agencies. One nice touch if you're not carrying to you can "fly" to the start space for $100 from anywhere on the board. There's even other odds and ends to purchase that can help you out (lawyers, fake id, "heater")

Play to a certain preset goal ($25,000 to $100,000, is suggested, but more or less will work).

Each player plays their favourite 'ganxta', tries to build their reputation, choose the correct tactics and to become the boss of the underworld at the end of the game.

The system is based on communication between the players.  The game board does not say whai happens, but what could happen.  Everything is decided by the result of a 'roshambo' with the other players.  You can try to guess your opponent's intentions, bargain with them, trick them, even bribe them.

The game takes place in four quarters, from the ghettos to the luxury of the choicest casino.

Further details on www.rebelshop.cz

Mind Acrobats: The players must find a way to the goal through the mind paths of the game board as quickly as possible.

Edition Spielbox #85 - from issue 5/2008

This French-language educational game consists of 16 sets of 3 tiles each depicting important French leaders from Vercingetorix to Napoleon III.  Each set of tiles displays the head (plus name and birth and death dates), torso, and legs of the leader in question.  All three cards are marked with the same identifying number (1-16).  The game also comes with a 4-page biographical survey of the depicted leaders.

For the under-6's, the game is simply one of putting the tiles together to form the leaders.  In the solitaire version, the child attempts to correctly construct all 16 figures. In the two-player version, children flip tiles one at a time by turn, trying to build the leaders.  The child with the most complete leaders wins.

The game for 6-and-overs plays with a simple Go-Fish mechanic where 2-3 players take turns trying to reconstruct the famous leaders of French history by asking each other for specific body parts and pulling from the draw pile if they don't get them.  Again, the winner is the player who has reconstituted the most historical personages before the draw pile is gone and someone has run out of tiles.

Part of the Munchkin series

The Wicked Munchkin Die is produced by Q-workshop—under license from Steve Jackson Games—for the game MUNCHKIN™.

The die itself is a large decahedron (d10) numbered “1” through “Ding!” with each face showing the iconic Munchkin character (artwork by John Kovalic) in ever increasing amounts of gear as he progresses from level 1 to level 10.

The die is available in a number of variations:

     Wicked Munchkin Die – Black line art on a white die.
     Wicked Munchkin Bag & Die – Adds a linen dice bag decorated with a coloured Munchkin character.
     Wicked Munchkin Die, Premium Hand Painted – Each face is hand-coloured by artists working with Q-workshop.
     Wicked Munchkin Die, Deluxe Hand Painted – Each face is hand-coloured by artists working with Q-workshop. The quality is better than the premium hand painted die.
     Wicked Munchkin Die, Holiday Edition – White line art on a red die.
     Wicked Munchkin Die (Pink) – Black line art on a pink die. This color originates when they switch production between white and red dyes. Very rare.
     Wicked Munchkin Die (Blue) – Blue line art on a white die.


				
				
					Special rulesGeneral rule:
If you are using a Wicked Munchkin Die, you start the game with one extra level.

Special rule: “Roll the Bones”
If you are using the Wicked Munchkin Die, and you are fighting an undead monster you may spend a level to roll that die. You get a bonus equal to the roll against that monster.

The first game release of Michael Schacht based on his 1995 finalist of the "Hippodice Autorenwettbewerb".

The game is about producing bands and releasing records. Depending on the positions in the charts the players get more or less fame and money.

The Munchkin Bobblehead adds a new rule to any game of Munchkin.  By having him present, you are able to re-roll one die per game.

				
				
					Publisher's DescriptionMunchkin has sold hundreds of thousands of copies, with more than a dozen different sequels and supplements, covering fantasy, sci-fi, chop-socky, horror, and more. With each set, the monsters get bigger, the curses stronger, and your party members no more trustworthy.

It's dangerous out there. You need a buddy.

The Munchkin Bobblehead will watch your back during your adventures, nodding encouragement to help you through the tough patches, and congratulating you on your treasure. Keep him on your desk at the office for inspiration - after all, what's the office but a dungeon with cubicles? The classic Munchkin figure, complete with horned helm, bag of loot, and enormous hammer (and his latest victim!) is a great way to spot your fellow Munchkin players.

But most of all, keep him around during your Munchkin games. Not only is he nifty in his own right... he gives you an advantage in play. And in Munchkin, you need every bonus you can get.

Munchkin Bobblehead - your nodding, bonus-giving, classic Munchkin buddy, 100% guaranteed to not stab you in the back.

				
				
					OtherPart of the Munchkin series.

'Little Game'

All players are editors of the Spielerei German Games magazine and they all want to show their 5 latest reviews to the chief editor.  But only the only review that will be printed is the one that is shown first to the chief editor.

boardgame: Nicke & Nilla: Spel & bus på vinden
description: The goal of the game is to collect "Nicke & Nilla" tokens. You gain tokens by following instructions from the cards, as well as challenge and win tokens from your fellow players.

Miffy (Nijntje) plays on the beach with her friends, collecting shells.

Three big Miffy "Meeples", in three different colors, are used to collect shells on the beach, using color-sided dice (D6).

a nice plus: the "meeples" can also be used to really play in the sand!

This set includes three types of dice: 5 poker dice, 5 regular dice, and a die showing various parts of the body.  Also in the package is a dice cup and a hardcover book that describes proper dice rolling etiquette, the distribution of outcomes (bell curve), and rules for 19 games many of which include a variation using the die with the body parts.

Diamond Dynasty includes two Gold Decks of Domino 32 cards, two Silver Decks of Domino 32 cards, a set of Play Money (80 pieces, 8 denominations), the Domino 32 Card Games booklet which includes the rules for 9 games, printed out rules for the games Twister: Dragon Dances and Diamond Dynasty along wit ha set of scoring sheets.

Bohnenspiel (Bean Game) is an old German game for two players that belongs to the Mancala family of pit and seeds games.

As for the other games in the Mancala Family, the goal of the game is to accumulate more seeds in player's storehouse than the opponent.

This is done by landing the last seed you are "sowing" in some pit bringing the total number of seeds in that pit to two, four or six. All previous pits containing two, four or six seeds are captured as well.

Muistiepeli is a memory game for Ages 6 - 10. It helps to learn also the basic childrens' rights listed by the United Nations. To aid with the learning, this game also has pictures and poems.

As in the basic memory game you try to find matching pairs. However in this game the pictures are different and the poems continue in the pairs.

The game includes two sets of rules. First is for the small children the second set is for the little older children.

boardgame: Star Wars Clone Wars: Sith gegen Jedi
description:
The republic is full of conflicts. One solar system after the other is turns into a battle field. In this card game you assume the role of a warrior in the star wars universe trying to survive by using your skills and a little bit of luck.

Box content:

99 cards in six colors
18 planet cards in six colors 
6 Coruscant-cards in 6 colors
6 stand-up characters (cardboard)

Basic childrens' game based on the Disney's Mickey Mouse Clubhouse television show.

From the box:
"Make a match with Mickey and friends.  Have a blast with the cast of the Mickey Mouse Clubhouse Show.  Match numbers on one side of the gameboard, then flip it over and see if you can match letters to spell the names of favorite characters.  Will you be the Hot Dog in this game?

Reinforces:
Number matching
Letter Recognition
Basic words

A game over the battle of Stirling 1297. 
William Wallace defeated the combined English forces of John de Warenne, 7th Earl of Surrey and Hugh de Cressingham
The game use the Sredniowiecze system and it has a 100/70 cm map, 240 counters. The rules are in Polish.

Crypt:  Monster and magic in a dungeon you create!

In Crypt, players take on the role of evil wizards who control competing monster forces, which move about in a dungeon that constantly grows.  Each turn, a player may take one of three actions:  lay a tile which expands the dungeon, move a monster in his force, or cast a magic spell.  The objective is to move your monsters into enemy Crypts.

Monsters begin the game in their home Crypt.  Each has a strength and movement rating.  Strong monsters are slow, weak ones fast.  Which will you play early and which will you save for later?  Which will you use to defend, and which will seek entry into enemy Crypts?

Dungeon tiles are used to build the dungeon.  Lay them carefully to block monsters from entering your Crypt, and make entryways into enemy Crypts.

Spell tiles keep things interesting.  Poisons lower abilities; Potions raise them.  A Reaper could mean instant death, while a Seraph returns you safely to your Crypt.  Traps stop movement, while a Ghost lets you walk through walls.  Or use Transporters to pop up in unexpected places.  Suffering under an enemy spell? Remove it with a Zapper.

Crypt is an easily-learned, quick-playing tile-laying game suitable for 2-4 players Ages 8 and up.  It is available as a self-published print-and-play game.

boardgame: The hand that feeds, the hand that beats
description:
The Hand That Feeds, The Hand That Beats.

Gods want power
... they'll do whatever it takes to get it...

In a world of faith, or better: a world of faiths, different followers worship different Gods.
The more faith and followers, the stronger a God is.
So they play their game of power and strength and sometimes just use the followers as playing pieces...

In The Hand that FEEDS, the Hand that BEATS, players represent a God and His followers. The priest of your faith
may call a creature that scoops up the whole village and moves them around the board. The followers can then be used
to acquire resources with which they build obilisks or the temple or a part of  one of the Gods' wonders: The Great Canal, The pyramid or the Mighty Colossus.

Three conditions trigger the game end:
1] If all three of the Gods' wonders are finished at the end of a round, the game ends.
2] If the creature-deck runs  out, the round is played to the end, after which the game ends.
3] After ten rounds, the game ends.

Players try  to score the most victory points (VPs).

     If they build or convert obelisks or the temple they immediately score VPs.
     If they build parts of the Gods' wonders, they can score VPs every round.
     If they control the obelisk in the oasis, they score VPs if other players use the oasis.
     Some of the actions, miracles and disasters will earn a player VPs.
     Lastly, and most importantly, the most popular faith will be elected to feed all the players and receives VPs equal to the popularity of the faith.

El Valle de la Muerte is a solitary game about the famous charge of the Light Brigade as a part of the fourth phase of the Battle of Balaclava on October the 25th of 1854.

The game simulates the twenty terrible minutes of the charge against the Russian cannons, the combat against the gunners, the Russian cavalry, and finally the retreat of embattled survivors who were pursued by the Russian until the British lines.

In the game, the principal characters of the Light Brigade are represented. As an example Lord Cardigan who rode in the front of lines, Captain Nolan who actively participated in the mistake that led the Light Brigade into Death Valley; Captain Morris of the 17th Lancers, etc. Details of the historical events are included, even the two dogs “Boxer” and “Jemmy” who were part of the charge are represented”.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Do you want to vote a Pope? Now it’s possible. Conclave recreates the event in which a new Pope is being elected. You represent one of the factions within the Vatican and your goal is to become the most powerful. 
During a conclave you know each player has to vote for a cardinal, but how will they vote? And how can you convince them how to vote? Who is your friend, who is your foe? Make your own plan, see what the others are up to, but most importantly, vote tactically and become the most powerful faction within the Vatican.

Conclave recreates the event in which a new pope is being elected. The factions of cardinals use their influence to make one of them the new Pope. You represent one of those factions and it is your task to gain the most influence, and to be the Pope-maker

This game have been created by two people who have time to waste on school day. A game which just needs : A pencil, a piece of sheet grid, a Friend and your Brains. Cause it's a strategic game, where you need to fight and conquer territory, by strangle your enemy in a prison of holes. Ok. Now here is the rule.

As told above, the only things you need, is Pencil and Sheet grid, That's right, no eraser just that. Its a game which is simple to do, to play, and to explain. Just few rules to understand and you can play.

What about the story ? Imagine that you are in a limited zone, with a chosen draw as player. You move and shoot each turn. When you move, you blacken your old player and place a new one at movement range. When you shoot, you blacken the place you shot from which is in Attack Range. A place which is Blackened cannot be passed through and is considered as an empty box. When the players ( all players ) aren't connected ( divided by empty boxes. ) The game ends, and the winner is the player with the most space. This game maybe looks  boring but it isn't!  Try it, its cost you nothing.

A lot of variants can be made, from the mines to Multiplication Artifact and Invisible Shield. Same for the Mod, Capture Flag, Zombie mod, Team, Werewolf. Infinite possibilities.

Pex is a two-player abstract board game played on a board with pentagonal tiling.

Both players place stones trying to form a chain connecting opposite sides of the board marked with their color.

This game is a close relative to Hex but the unique tiling makes the game strategies different.

Godzilla: Monsters Attack! is a co-operative game based on the Godzilla franchise.  At the start of the game, two monsters are chosen from a collection of monsters from the Godzilla universe.  During the game, the monsters randomly move around the board fighting each other, fighting military units, and destroying cities.  The players lose if the monsters cause too much destruction.  To prevent this, players need to move around the board investigating various locations to uncover a plot.  Once the players have done enough investigating, one of numerous plot cards is drawn.  The plot provides a win condition for the players but also makes the monsters more powerful.  The players must then race to meet the win condition before the monsters destroy Japan.  Aside from uncovering and resolving a plot, players may also build and mobilize military units to prevent the monsters from damaging the cities and buy valuable time.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
From the rule book cover:

"EDUCATIONAL
Learn business terms and practices...
stock market, high finance, bartering...
Become wiser by trading and manipulating...
A game of skill combined with chance...

ENTERTAINING
Interesting to young and old...
Delightfully amusing...
Every game different...
One throw of the dice and you may "gain" a hotel or "lose" your shirt...
but one thing is sure that everyone will enjoy the game..."

Inside Track is a for 3-6 players based on business principles of purchasing, leasing, and bartering.  Play progresses with players taking leases on different businesses, each with different income properties.  Play continues, until all but two players are bankrupt.  At that time players have a final audit of the last two players to determine the winner.  The object of the game is to accumulate the greatest wealth (leased property and money) at the final audit.

Expansion for the 2007 Ystari Game "Caylus Magna Carta" (from Spielbox 2/2007).

Whoever builds the library may take the first card from their draw pile (in phase 1 - income) each round.

Note:

    This card is included with the Rio Grande Games edition of the base game

Hexe Lilli is a famous German children‘s book character. 
In the game Hexe Lilli (witch Lilli) makes a big chaos when performing magic. The players have to keep in mind where the different sized bewitched things are.

Allways wanted to A-bomb Norway and steamroll and 
put tarmac all over it, including the cities and the civilians?

NUKE NORWAY NOW / Handmade not mass produced version.
English version officially released at Essen2009 together with NNN "the soundtrack"

Tactical & strategical boardgame for 2players 
( includes a 3player scenario also)
